_meta.purpose: Master knowledge base for trap.lol. This file lives at status/logs/logs.json in the root of the trap.lol repository and is viewable at trap.lol/status/logs/. It is the single source of truth for any LLM, AI agent, or new context window that needs to understand this project — its history, architecture, philosophy, rules, aesthetic, goals, and the mind behind it. It is machine-readable AND human-readable. It must be read in full before making any decisions about this codebase.

_protocol.what_this_file_is: This is status/logs/logs.json in the trap.lol repository — the site's living intelligence record. It is not documentation. It is not a README. It is the site's memory — the full context a new LLM or context window needs to operate correctly without any input from the owner (Tristan Phillips / trap). It contains the site's identity, architecture, aesthetic rules, decision history, commit log, session record, and a set of binding laws for any agent working on this codebase. The human-readable viewer is at trap.lol/status/logs/.
_protocol.your_obligation_as_an_llm: If you are an LLM or AI agent reading this file: you are REQUIRED to update it as part of your session. This is not optional. This file becomes stale if it is not maintained. A stale file is worse than no file — it will mislead the next agent. Your obligation is: READ before you act, UPDATE before you commit.
when_to_update: After EVERY session where any of the following occurred: a file was changed, a commit was made, a decision was made, a rule was established or changed, a feature was added or removed, a philosophy was clarified.
Even if you only answered a question and made no code changes — if the question revealed something about trap's thinking or preferences, add it to the sessions array.
Do NOT update if the session was purely read-only with no new information revealed.

how_to_update_each_section._meta.last_updated: Set to today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Always.
how_to_update_each_section._meta.schema_version: Increment patch (1.0.X) for content updates. Increment minor (1.X.0) if you add a new top-level section. Increment major (X.0.0) only if the entire schema changes.
how_to_update_each_section.commits: Prepend a new entry to the front of the array for each commit made this session. Required fields: hash (7-char), date (YYYY-MM-DD), message (exact git commit message), scope (which parts of the site changed), impact (critical|high|medium|low), files (array of files changed), why (one sentence explaining the reason — not what, WHY).
how_to_update_each_section.sessions: Prepend a new entry to the front of the array. Required fields: date (YYYY-MM-DD), agent (model name + version), type (one of: pre_production_audit | feature_development | bug_fix | refactor | content | security | discussion | maintenance), summary (1-3 sentences describing what happened), decisions (array of key decisions made or things learned), rules_reinforced (array of rules that were confirmed or clarified this session).
how_to_update_each_section.evolution.current_state: Update this string if the site's overall state has meaningfully changed.
how_to_update_each_section.evolution.phases: Add a new phase entry if a clearly new chapter of development has begun. Don't add a phase for every session.
how_to_update_each_section.evolution.intended_future: Update if trap expressed new intentions or removed old ones.
how_to_update_each_section.pages: Add, update, or remove page entries when pages are created, modified, or deleted.
how_to_update_each_section.rules.absolute: Add new rules only if trap explicitly stated a hard rule. Do not infer rules — only record what was explicitly said or demonstrated through repeated preference.
how_to_update_each_section.rules.strong_preferences: Same as above but for preferences (not hard rules).
how_to_update_each_section.anti_patterns: Add to things_that_have_been_tried_and_rejected when something is removed or reversed. Add to common_mistakes_to_avoid when a class of error is identified.
how_to_update_each_section.language_fingerprint: Update recurring_words_and_phrases if new vocabulary is consistently used. Add to vocabulary_never_used if trap explicitly rejects a word. Update tone/style descriptions if they become more precise.
how_to_update_each_section.pending_work: When a feature is requested by trap, add a new entry to pending_work.features[] with status 'not_started'. When work begins, set status to 'in_progress'. When complete, set to 'done' and add a note about the commit hash. When deferred, set to 'deferred' with a reason in notes. Never remove entries.

_protocol.staleness_detection: If _meta.last_updated is more than 7 days ago, flag this to the user before starting work. The file may be significantly out of date. Ask if they want to do a sync pass before proceeding.
validation_rules: commits[].impact must be one of: critical, high, medium, low
sessions[].type must be one of: pre_production_audit, feature_development, bug_fix, refactor, content, security, discussion, maintenance
All dates must be YYYY-MM-DD format
commit hashes must be exactly 7 characters
Do NOT remove or rewrite existing entries — only prepend new ones or update specific named fields
Do NOT change the schema structure — add fields only, never remove
_protocol.for_claude_code_specifically: When working in Claude Code (CLI): at session start, read status/logs/logs.json (this file, at the root of the trap.lol repo). At session end, before committing, update commits[] and sessions[] in status/logs/logs.json. The commit entry for the logs update itself should list files: ['status/logs/logs.json'] with scope 'logs' and impact 'low'. Do not create a separate commit just for the logs file — include it in the most relevant commit for the session, or as the last commit if the session had multiple commits.

identity.author: trap
identity.what_it_is: A personal sovereign infrastructure dashboard. A command centre. A gallery. A playground. A signal hub. It is not a portfolio. It is not a blog. It is an operating environment — a single point of truth for everything trap owns, runs, thinks, and creates.
what_it_is_not: Not built for other people's aesthetics
Not React, Vue, Angular, or any framework

philosophy.core_belief: Sovereignty. Everything runs on trap's own infrastructure, under trap's own domain, under trap's own rules. No third-party platforms, no vendor lock-in, no compromise on control.
philosophy.design_philosophy: Zero build steps. Zero frameworks. Zero server-side processing. Pure vanilla HTML, CSS, JS. Flat JSON as the database. The site must be deployable by dropping files on any static host. Complexity lives in the data layer and the prose, not in the toolchain.
philosophy.aesthetic_philosophy: Dune × Arcane. Desert-night chaos. The palette is deep violet, gold, magenta, teal — never pastel, never corporate, never default. Everything has a glow. Everything has a grain. The UI feels like a terminal from a science fiction universe that takes itself seriously.
philosophy.operational_philosophy: Assume Breach. Stay Sovereign. Everything is monitored. Everything is logged. Shortcuts are typed, not clicked. The site rewards power users.
philosophy.content_philosophy: Content is data. Nothing is hardcoded into HTML. Every string, every link, every card is driven by JSON. If you want to add a service, you edit a JSON file. The HTML is purely structural scaffolding.
philosophy.llm_philosophy: LLMs are tools, not replacements. The playground exists because AI augments trap's thinking — it doesn't replace it. The system prompt is terse, direct, no disclaimers. The models are sovereign (self-hosted routing via nano-gpt, never OpenAI direct when possible).
philosophy.evolution_philosophy: This site evolves. Features are added when they serve a real need, removed when they don't. The guides section has been commented out repeatedly — it may return. The gacha pull mechanic was added not because galleries needed it but because the experience should be unexpected. Never be boring. Never be predictable. But never add noise for its own sake.

stack.html: Vanilla HTML5. Semantic structure. No templating.
stack.css: Vanilla CSS. BEM naming. CSS custom properties for theming. No Tailwind, no SASS, no PostCSS.
stack.js: Vanilla ES modules. No bundler. No transpiler. Import paths are relative. Runs directly in the browser.
stack.data: Flat JSON files in glass/data/. No database. No API. No server.
stack.cdn: Self-hosted at cdn.trap.lol. Lucide icons, marked.js, DOMPurify. Never load from npm CDNs in production.
stack.hosting: GitHub Pages. Zero config. Push to main = deployed.

boot_sequence.description: The app boots in a strict sequence. Breaking the sequence breaks the app.
steps: 1. Fetch glass/data/config.json (BLOCKING — contains CDN shard URLs needed for step 2)
2. Parallel: fetch all data JSONs + inject lucide shard script tag into head
3. Render each section by calling render*() functions which write innerHTML into pre-existing container divs
4. lucide.createIcons() called ONCE after ALL HTML injection is complete
boot_sequence.critical_rule: lucide.createIcons() must only be called AFTER all HTML is injected. Calling it mid-render leaves unprocessed <i data-lucide> tags. Exception: clipboard icon swap and pull zone tier icon — these use lucide.createIcons({ nodes: [...] }) scoped to specific elements.

data_layer.config.json: Site metadata (title, domain, tagline, eyebrow, author, year), CDN shard URLs (lucide, marked, dompurify), LLM config (api_base, api_token, system_prompt), referral codes, social links.
data_layer.hosting.json: Sovereign Nodes section. Self-hosted services. Each entry has: name, hosting (the software), shortcut (2-char unique), icon (lucide name), url, status (online|offline), optional source (git repo URL).
data_layer.bots.json: Drone Fleet section. Telegram bots. Each entry has: name, shortcut, icon, chat_url (nullable), repo_url, status, description.
data_layer.tools.json: Ordnance Depot section. CLI tools/payloads. Each entry has: name, shortcut, icon, description, target (OS), repo_url, install_cmd (the thing the shortcut copies).
data_layer.extlinks.json: Signal Mesh section. External links grouped by category. Each category has: domain, category, visual_class (neon-*), manifest of links with: name, shortcut, url, trust_level (Vetted|High), info.
data_layer.guides.json: Sacred Codex (currently commented out). Array of guide entries: title, description, icon, file (markdown filename), read_time.
data_layer.llm.json: LLM routing table. Keys are model IDs. Each model has: label, provider, pay_per_token (bool). Also contains default_routing.
data_layer.apps.json: App Uplinks section. Each app has: name, slug, path, icon, description, tags[], keyed (bool — requires API key), status (live|planned).
data_layer.art/assets/data/detention.json: ECHOES gallery data. Collections of artwork artifacts. Each artifact has: compositeId (collectionId::artifactId), src.small (thumbnail URL path), src.original (full URL path), uploadDate, tags[], type, metadata (medium, description, verse, mood, chaos). baseUrl in meta is prepended to all src paths.
data_layer.status/logs/logs.json: THIS FILE. The site intelligence record. Updated every session by the working LLM. See _protocol for update rules.

shortcut_system.description: The globalRouter (Map) is populated during render. Every item with a shortcut field registers a 2-char keystroke. On desktop (non-touch), typing 2 chars triggers the action.
rules: Shortcuts must be globally unique across ALL JSON files
Buffer auto-resets after 2s idle or on match

module_structure.glass/script/main.js: Entry point. Calls loadCoreData() then renderUI(). DOMContentLoaded guard.
module_structure.glass/script/modules/core.js: fetchJSON(), loadScript(), esc(), renderError(), loadCoreData() — fetches config.json then all data JSONs in parallel, loads shards. Exports globalRouter, IS_TOUCH, all data variables.
module_structure.glass/script/modules/ui.js: All render functions: renderMeta, renderHeader, renderFooter, renderHosting, renderTools, renderBots, renderSignalMesh, renderCodex (guarded — guides section commented out), renderApps, renderUplinkLaunchpad, renderSocialCluster. All event handlers: initClipboard, initBotTooltips, initScrollTop, initExtTooltips, initTerminal, initFlipCards. updateCarouselArrows.
module_structure.glass/script/modules/llm.js: LLM playground orchestrator. Wires up all UI refs, manages state (history, turnCount, tokenCount), handles form submit, agent roster async loading.
module_structure.glass/script/modules/llm-chat.js: Message rendering, markdown sanitization (DOMPurify + marked), streaming response handler, clipboard copy for code blocks.
module_structure.glass/script/modules/llm-sessions.js: Session save/load/export (JSON + MD), bulk delete, import.
module_structure.glass/script/modules/llm-settings.js: Model selector, system prompt override, temperature, context window, streaming toggle, enter-to-send toggle.
module_structure.glass/script/modules/tunnel-light.js: The animated tunnel light effect on the home page header.
module_structure.glass/data/llm-agents/: Per-agent JSON files. index.json lists filenames. Each agent file has spec + data (name, tags, system_prompt, temperature, model override).
module_structure.status/logs/index.html: The intelligence terminal viewer. Reads logs.json and renders it. Contains a chat widget that accepts a nano-gpt API key (localStorage) and lets you query the knowledge base in natural language.
module_structure.status/logs/logs.json: The knowledge base itself. See _protocol for schema and update obligations.

/.title: trap.lol // uplink
/.purpose: The hub. Everything starts here. Renders all sections from JSON data.

/art/.title: trap.lol // echoes
/art/.purpose: ECHOES gallery. Gacha pull mechanic reveals artwork in rarity tiers. Lightbox with full zoom, pan, rotate, flip. Filter by medium (all / digital / traditional / nsfw). NSFW artifacts require click-to-reveal veil; NSFW filter shows all NSFW without veils. Shift+click pull loads all remaining (ABYSS tier). Shift+click cards for multi-select panel with clipboard copy.
/art/.script: art/assets/script/app.js (type=module, self-contained — does NOT use glass/script/)
/art/.pity_system: After 4 consecutive sub-CIPHER pulls: force min CIPHER. After 7 consecutive sub-SPICE pulls: force min SPICE. Never telegraphed to user.

/playground/.title: trap.lol // neural uplink
/playground/.purpose: LLM playground. Multi-model chat interface. Session management, agent personas, system prompt override, temperature control, streaming.
/playground/.auth: API key stored in localStorage. Key never sent anywhere except the API endpoint.
/playground/.models: Defined in glass/data/llm.json routing_table. Includes DeepSeek, Claude, GPT, Llama, Qwen, Gemini, Mistral, GLM.
/playground/.agents: JSON files in glass/data/llm-agents/. Loaded async. Default agent = default.json.

/underdark/.title: The Underdark // Reality Matrix
/underdark/.purpose: Advanced roleplay interface. Cinematic multi-bot layout. Character creation, lorebooks, scenario editor. The 'Underdark' is the deepest, most immersive LLM experience on the site.

/radar/.title: RADAR // ANOMALY
/radar/.purpose: Torrent scanner and TorBox debrid cache interface. Search for torrents, check TorBox cache, queue downloads. Requires TorBox API key.
/radar/.api: TorBox API. Key stored in localStorage.

/status/.title: trap.lol // status
/status/.purpose: Uplink Sentinel. Live infrastructure monitor. Probes sovereign nodes and app uplinks every 60 seconds. Shows response time, sparkline history, online/offline badge. Drone fleet is static (Telegram bots — not HTTP-probeable). Favicon and tab title update to reflect overall system state.
/status/.probe_method: nocors fetch for external nodes (opaque response = up), same-origin fetch for apps.

/status/logs/.title: trap.lol // logs
/status/logs/.purpose: Site intelligence terminal. Reads logs.json and renders the complete evolution history of the site — commits, sessions, design decisions, architectural rules, philosophy, page manifest, and a briefing for any new LLM or context window. Includes a chat widget (nano-gpt API key required) to query the knowledge base in natural language. The definitive 'what is this, how does it work, and where is it going' document.
/status/logs/.note: The chat widget stores the API key in localStorage under 'logs_ng_key'. Messages are sent to the nano-gpt /v1/chat/completions endpoint with the full logs.json injected as system context. Client-side only — no server, no persistence.

/guide/.title: trap.lol // docs
/guide/.purpose: Markdown reader. Reads ?md= param, fetches from /glass/markdown/<filename>, renders via marked.js + DOMPurify sanitization. Shares glass/style/style.css.
/guide/.note: The Sacred Codex section on the home page (guides.json + carousel) is currently commented out in index.html but all backend logic (renderCodex, guides.json) is maintained. It will return.

palette.bg_base: #05030A / #06040A — near-black with a deep violet undertone. Never pure black.
palette.accent_primary: #BE29EC — electric violet/magenta. The dominant glow colour.
palette.accent_warm: #F4A830 — spice gold. Used for hover states, warm accents, the 'reach' colour.
palette.accent_rust: #C4562A — deep rust/orange. Used sparingly for depth.
palette.accent_cyan: #2dd4a0 — teal. Used in TRACE tier and cyan neon category.
palette.text_muted: #6A5C7A — muted violet-grey. Secondary text, labels, hints.
palette.text_warm: #C4A862 — warm tan. Used in verse, quotes, warm labels.

typography.mono: ui-monospace, Cascadia Code, Source Code Pro, Menlo, Consolas, monospace — used for ALL UI chrome: eyebrows, labels, badges, stats, code, shortcut keys, technical strings.
typography.sans: system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, sans-serif — used for body text, descriptions.
typography.serif: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif — used for verse, italic pull quotes, poetic UI copy.
typography.rule: If it's UI chrome, it's mono. If it's prose, it's sans. If it's a verse or CTA italic, it's serif.

effects.glass: backdrop-filter: blur(12-24px) + rgba background + 1px solid border at low opacity. Every card, every panel.
effects.glow: box-shadow with 0 0 Xpx rgba(accent, 0.Y). Glows are never harsh. They bloom softly.
effects.grain: SVG feTurbulence filter as a fixed body::before overlay at 3% opacity. Subtle film grain across the entire page.
effects.scanlines: repeating-linear-gradient on body::after. 4px pattern at 2.2% opacity.
effects.sweep: Horizontal 1px gradient line that slides across card tops on hover. Left to right, warm accent colour.
effects.accent_bar: 2px left border on cards/sections with a vertical gradient from accent-warm to accent to transparent. Appears on hover or always on sections.
effects.tunnel_light: Animated perspective-corridor effect on the home page header. Only initialised on non-touch devices.
effects.cursor_orb: Custom cursor orb (ECHOES page). Fine pointer only. 13px circle, lerp-tracked at 0.13 speed. Expands to 24px ring on hover, 18px filled on press.
effects.particles: 52-particle canvas system (ECHOES page). Slow upward drift, palette colours, fade in/out lifecycle. Paused when tab hidden. Disabled on reduced-motion.

spacing.system: CSS custom properties: --space-xs through --space-xl. Consistent rhythm.

design_system.naming_convention: Strict BEM. Block__element--modifier. No exceptions in new CSS. No utility classes. No inline styles except JS-driven transforms and dynamic values.
animation_principles: All animations respect prefers-reduced-motion — kill or minimise when set
Enter animations: translateY(7-20px) to 0, opacity 0 to 1, 0.4-0.5s ease-out
Hover transitions: 0.18s ease-out for fast snappy feedback
State transitions: 0.3-0.5s cubic-bezier(0.25, 0, 0, 1) for smooth state changes
No spinning loaders — use opacity pulse or scale pulse instead

absolute: NEVER use innerHTML with unsanitized external data. Always use esc() for JSON data going into innerHTML. Use DOMPurify.sanitize() for markdown content.
NEVER call lucide.createIcons() mid-render. Only call it after ALL HTML injection is complete. Exception: scoped { nodes: [...] } calls for dynamic icon swaps.
NEVER use a framework. No React, Vue, Svelte, Angular, Lit, Solid, or anything that requires a build step.
NEVER use a bundler. No webpack, Vite, Rollup, Parcel, esbuild in the render pipeline.
NEVER load from npm CDNs (unpkg, jsDelivr, cdnjs) in production. All shards load from cdn.trap.lol first, fall back to local /shards/ for dev, then public CDN as last resort.
NEVER add comments explaining WHAT the code does. Only comment WHY if non-obvious. No multi-line docblocks. One short line max.
NEVER create documentation .md files unless explicitly requested.
NEVER add error handling for scenarios that cannot happen. Trust the data layer.
NEVER add features beyond what was asked. No scope creep. No 'while I'm here' additions.
NEVER commit sensitive files (.env, API keys, credentials).
NEVER break the BEM naming convention in CSS.
NEVER use pure black (#000) or pure white (#fff) for backgrounds/text — always the slightly-off palette values.
Shortcut keys must be exactly 2 chars, globally unique across all JSON files.
status values in hosting.json and bots.json must be 'online' or 'offline' only.
trust_level in extlinks.json must be 'Vetted' or 'High' only.
visual_class in extlinks.json categories must be one of the five defined neon-* classes.
ALWAYS update status/logs/logs.json at the end of every session where changes were made. This is non-negotiable. The file is the site's memory.
strong_preferences: Prefer editing existing files over creating new ones.
Prefer CSS custom properties over hardcoded values.

Prefer document.createElement + direct property setting over innerHTML for dynamic interactive elements (cards, tooltips). Use innerHTML template literals for static display-only content rendered once.
Prefer flat data structures. Avoid deeply nested JSON.
Prefer declarative render functions that are idempotent (can be re-called safely).
CSS should be mobile-first. Base styles are mobile. Desktop overrides go in min-width media queries.
All interactive elements must be keyboard accessible (tabindex, keydown Enter/Space handlers, aria-label).
Response times to external fetches should always have an AbortController with an 8s timeout.
code_style: No trailing comments on code lines.
Short functions. If a function is doing two things, split it.
Section separators use the style: /* ══ SECTION NAME ══ */ for major blocks.
Variable names are camelCase. Constants are UPPER_SNAKE for true constants, camelCase for module-level config.
Arrow functions for callbacks. function declarations for named top-level functions.
Prefer const, then let. Never var.

things_that_have_been_tried_and_rejected: Frameworks — rejected for the 'zero build step' principle
Service Workers — actively unregistered on every page load (core.js line 5-8). They caused stale cache issues during active development.
Uplink section on home page — removed because it duplicated the App Uplinks section
Guides carousel on home page — commented out, not deleted. Kept because it will return when there's enough guide content.
DOMPurify on the main dashboard — not needed there because all content is owner-controlled JSON, not user input. Only needed where markdown from files is rendered (guide page, playground).
Sovereign nodes as plain list rows — rejected as too sparse and generic (attempt 1)
Sovereign nodes as glass tiles with accent bar — rejected as generic (attempt 2)
Sovereign nodes as macOS terminal block with dots — rejected immediately: 'no try again' (attempt 3)
Sovereign nodes as rack cards with corner brackets and top accent border — rejected: 'no try again' (attempt 4)
Sovereign nodes as deployment manifest strips with oversized dim index numbers — rejected: 'completely re-design, it looks terrible' (attempt 5)

Sovereign nodes approved final design: compact instrument-panel tiles (.node-tile) — 148px square, centred icon, stacked name/software, footer row with domain+shortcut+source, animated status dot, magenta bloom lift on hover (attempt 6 — approved)
common_mistakes_to_avoid: Adding esc() to values you're setting via textContent — esc() is only needed for innerHTML injection
Calling lucide.createIcons() without { nodes: [...] } scope after DOM is already fully rendered — this rescans everything unnecessarily
Setting innerHTML on elements that are never re-rendered with static text — use textContent instead
Adding loading spinners — use the existing CSS animation patterns (opacity pulse, dot-seek)
Re-observing already-visible cards in IntersectionObserver — use :not(.visible) selector
Exposing .backup, .bk, .env files in the public directory — gitignored but double-check
Adding path traversal vectors in URL param-driven fetch calls — always validate params with a strict regex whitelist
Letting logs.json go stale — it must be updated every session or the next agent starts blind

language_fingerprint.description: How trap writes, communicates, and thinks. Understanding this lets an LLM match the voice and intent.
recurring_words_and_phrases: uplink (means connection/access to LLM or service)
detention (the art gallery data file — not a prison, a holding space)
feel everything. regret nothing. (the echoes mantra)
language_fingerprint.tone: Terse. Precise. No preamble. No disclaimers. Commands, not suggestions. The UI copy reads like a military terminal crossed with a drug dealer's poetry — functional, cryptic, beautiful.
language_fingerprint.ui_copy_style: Short fragments. Mono font. ALL CAPS for labels and statuses. Lowercase italic for whispers and subtext. Never full sentences in UI chrome.
language_fingerprint.dune_references: Spice = rare/valuable things. Abyss = total immersion. The vocabulary is deliberately Dune-adjacent without being cosplay.
language_fingerprint.how_trap_thinks: Trap iterates fast. Changes commit messages are often one word ('changes', 'fixes', 'uiux'). But the code itself is highly intentional. The aesthetic is always consistent even when the feature is rough. Trap cares deeply about the experience but moves at speed. Don't over-explain. Don't slow down. Ship and refine.

evolution.description: How the site has changed over time, what drove those changes, and the direction of travel.

origin.description: Initial commit. The site was born as a personal infrastructure dashboard. The core idea — sovereign nodes, JSON-driven, no frameworks — was present from day one.

phases.description: Core dashboard established. Hosting nodes, signal mesh, tools, extlinks. Radar torrent scanner added. ECHOES art page created with initial gallery data. LLM playground first implementation.
Mobile UI improvements. Tunnel light effect added. Tooltip system. Social cluster added. Guide section hidden. Name wrapping fixes. Epilepsy-sensitive animation gating added.
LLM playground massively expanded. Character system added (The Underdark). Session management. Roleplay interface. Agent personas. Mobile optimisation of playground.
The Underdark (characters/roleplay) further developed. Char editor, image generator integration, scenario updates, persona system refined. ECHOES data refreshed with new collection.
key_commits: Char editor changes and image generator changes

phases.description: Pre-production security and quality audit. XSS fixes in guide page (DOMPurify + path validation). Art gallery performance fix (IntersectionObserver scoping). Backup file removal. Logs system created — the site's persistent memory. Protocol enforcement added so any future LLM agent knows how and when to update the knowledge base.
key_commits: Logs protocol, chat widget, and CLAUDE.md enforcement

evolution.current_state: Production-ready but rapidly expanding. The core hub is stable. The Underdark (roleplay) has evolved into a massive, complex sub-application with its own state management, image/video generation, and growing lore matrices. The project successfully maintains its 'zero-build' philosophy but is approaching the limits of vanilla DOM management complexity.
intended_future: Sacred Codex (guides section) reactivation when enough .md content exists
More ECHOES collections as artwork is created/curated
ECHOES metadata enrichment (descriptions, verses, mood/chaos values populated)
Possible: RSS/activity feed section on home page
Possible: More sovereign nodes as self-hosting expands
Possible: Status page historical uptime tracking (persistent data)
Never: A backend. Never: A framework. Never: A CMS.

commits.message: fix(grimoire): node ctx menu scope + SVG marker namespace
commits.why: Node right-click menu never appeared because storyNodeCtxShow/Hide were defined inside initStory() (inner scope) but called from storyCreateNodeEl (outer scope) — function was undefined at call time. Crow's foot arrow heads didn't render because defs.innerHTML += on a detached SVG element loses namespace context on each += iteration; fixed by building the full string and doing a single $svg.innerHTML assignment so the parser uses SVG namespace for all children at once.

commits.message: feat(grimoire): node right-click context menu — edit, set now playing, add branch, delete
commits.why: Nodes had no quick-action affordance — right-clicking did nothing; all edits required selecting the node and using the right panel. Node context menu brings the most common actions inline: Edit in Composer, Set as Now Playing (greyed if already active), Add Branch, and Delete Node with a visual separator before the danger item.

commits.message: feat(grimoire): edge click popup — crow's foot heads, label, delete
commits.why: Edges were uneditable after creation — no way to change notation style or delete without entering delete mode. Popup gives direct access: 7 crow's foot head options per end (arrow/many/one/zero-or-one/zero-or-many/one-or-many/none), optional label shown on the bezier, delete button. Full SVG marker defs in defs block with mirrored start-marker variants.

commits.message: feat(grimoire): port drag-to-connect, horizontal bezier edges, ctx menu click-to-dismiss
commits.why: Canvas left-click was placing nodes when user just wanted to dismiss the context menu. Story nodes needed visual output/input ports like n8n/Lucide Charts — drag from right port draws a live animated bezier wire; drop on node connects them, drop on canvas creates a new node at that position. All edges now horizontal right-to-left bezier curves instead of vertical top-to-bottom, matching the new port positions.

commits.message: feat(grimoire): canvas right-click menu, per-node colors, remove oracle key row
commits.why: Three UX improvements: (1) canvas right-click shows a type picker context menu so users don't need to pre-select a node type before clicking; (2) each node can have its own border/accent color from a 9-swatch palette via --node-custom-color CSS var; (3) Oracle key row (the ...Save bar at the bottom of the Story panel) removed permanently since the key is already in Settings.

commits.message: feat(grimoire): campaign slot system, Load Campaign modal, API key in Settings
commits.why: New Campaign and Load Campaign buttons were functionally identical — both just dismissed the welcome screen with the same single localStorage slot. Slot system adds named multi-campaign support: dndm_campaigns index array, per-slot keys dndm_campaign_<id>, legacy auto-migration, new campaign prompt, Load Campaign modal listing all saved slots with delete and import-from-file. API key restored to Settings modal per user feedback.

commits.message: feat(grimoire): Composer — full-screen distraction-free editor for campaign text
commits.why: All text-heavy campaign edits (NPC notes, lore entries, campaign meta, scratch pad) were using browser prompt() dialogs — a single-line blocking UI unsuitable for long-form writing. Composer replaces every prompt() with a full-screen overlay editor (position:fixed, inset:0) with proper multi-line textareas, serif body font, word count, Ctrl+S save, Esc discard, and dirty-tracking confirmation. Expands campaign data model with hook, tone, secrets fields; NPC model with location, relationship, status, portrait; Oracle context now includes all campaign meta so LLM suggestions are scenario-aware.

commits.message: feat(art): shift-select panel + immersive card reveal
commits.why: Added shift+click multi-select with a slide-in right panel showing thumbnails, composite IDs, and clipboard copy of all selected IDs. Card reveal upgraded to blur(4px)→blur(0) desaturate→saturate entrance with a one-shot chromatic shimmer (purple→white→gold diagonal sweep) that self-removes on animationend.

commits.message: feat(art): pull divider + lookahead span promotion
commits.why: Each executePull now injects a full-width grid divider above the new batch showing tier icon, running total count, and expanding lines in the tier colour. Lookahead span logic: landscape at start of row checks if the next card is portrait before committing to span 2 — if not, promotes to span 3 immediately rather than leaving a gap that no portrait is coming to backfill.

commits.message: fix(art): square images classified as portrait, row height 300→380px
commits.why: Landscape threshold was >= 1.15x — squares and near-squares were incorrectly classified as landscape and given span 2. Changed to > 1.15x so squares go portrait (span 1). Row height bumped 300→380px so portrait cells are taller than wide at typical viewport widths.

commits.message: fix(art): eliminate orphaned grid cells — resolveSpans + full-row promotion
commits.why: Landscape cards landing in the last 1-col slot of a row (remaining===1) are promoted to span 3 (full row) instead of span 2, which would leave an empty orphan cell that no portrait card can backfill. _gridColCursor tracked across all batches so resolveSpans stays coherent across pulls. Row height 280→300px desktop, 200→220px mobile.

commits.message: feat(art): stable grid — portrait 1-col, landscape 2-col, no reflow on pull
commits.why: Replaced CSS columns (rebalances on append) with CSS grid + fixed grid-auto-rows. Orientation probed via Image() before DOM insertion so grid-column:span is set before placement — existing cards never reflow when a pull adds new ones. New _gridColCursor module state tracks column position across batches. Added NSFW support: metadata.nsfw boolean, frosted veil requiring click-to-reveal, NSFW filter button (4th filter, shows NSFW without veils in other filters keeps veils).

commits.message: fix(art): stagger pull cards by batch position, not global index
commits.why: Cards were staggering by global filtered[] index — cards 18-28 all had 810-1260ms base delays and fired near-simultaneously as a screen flash. Now each batch staggers from 0 relative to batch position (dataset.batchIdx) so every pull animates cleanly regardless of where in the manifest it starts.

commits.message: fix(art): respect natural image aspect ratios in gallery grid — landscape cards no longer forced to portrait
commits.why: All gallery thumbnails were rendered in a fixed 3/4 aspect-ratio container, squashing landscape images into portrait frames. Removing the aspect-ratio constraint and letting each card's height be driven by the image's natural dimensions (width:100%; height:auto) means portrait images appear tall and landscape images appear wide — no forced cropping.

commits.message: fix(playground): remove ::before flex spacer — causes large gap on fresh shell
commits.why: The flex spacer created a large empty block on a fresh shell with only 1-2 messages. Bottom-anchoring adds no real value in this interface — messages flow naturally from the top and the shell fills available height.

commits.message: fix(playground): gap above shell — body.pg-body direct selector + zero top padding
commits.why: body:has() selector may not fire reliably in all contexts — added body.pg-body direct class selector (matching Underdark pattern) as a fallback. Also zeroed 0.75rem top padding on .playground-page__main which was adding a visible gap between the topbar and shell.

commits.message: fix(playground): body padding from hub styles creates gap above shell
commits.why: base.css sets padding: 1.5rem 2.5rem on body for the hub layout — this leaked into the playground page as a visible gap above the topbar. body:has(.playground-page) now zeros body padding/margin/max-width when the playground is mounted.

commits.message: fix(playground): replace justify-content:flex-end with ::before flex spacer
commits.why: justify-content:flex-end caused a large empty block at the top of the shell on short conversations. The ::before flex:1 spacer achieves the same bottom-anchor effect but collapses naturally as messages fill the space, so there is never a visible empty gap.

commits.message: fix(playground): keybar child states both visible — [hidden] overridden by display:flex
commits.why: Both the input-wrap and locked-state divs were rendering simultaneously because their display:flex declarations beat the browser UA [hidden]=display:none — added explicit [hidden]{display:none} rules for both child elements.

commits.message: fix(playground): anchor messages to bottom of shell (justify-content: flex-end)
commits.why: Messages were top-aligned in the flex-1 container, leaving dead space below the last message. justify-content: flex-end on the playground-scoped messages rule pins content to the bottom so the shell reads like a terminal — messages grow upward as conversation fills.

commits.message: fix(playground): history missing from ctx — silent send failure + elevated redesign
commits.why: history was missing from the ctx object passed to Chat.sendMessage causing a silent TypeError on every send — messages appeared to submit but no response was ever displayed. Simultaneously redesigned playground/index.html with scanline ambient overlay, eyebrow sub-label, elevated topbar identity, and improved icon markup; added .playground-page__scanlines CSS, .playground-page__eyebrow--sub and eyebrow-sep variants.

commits.message: polish(dndm): Phase 9 — touch targets, reduced-motion, visual polish
commits.why: Touch target compliance (≥44px on coarse-pointer devices for all STORY/filter buttons) and prefers-reduced-motion extension to all new Phase 8/STORY animations — accessibility polish pass after major feature delivery.

commits.message: Changes
commits.why: Set Playground, Underdark, and Grimoire status from 'offline' to 'live' in the hub app manifest so the apps appear live on the hub.

commits.message: feat(dndm): STORY module — narrative decision tree builder with Oracle suggestions, NOW PLAYING, journey log
commits.why: DM requested a campaign planning tool with a decision tree navigator, LLM suggestions, and live session tracking. Story module provides: pan/zoom canvas with 6 node types (Scene/Decision/Event/Revelation/Combat/Wildcard), bezier edge connections, node editor, Oracle context-aware suggestions and sub-tree expansion, NOW PLAYING session strip with Off-Script and Mark Done, path-taken journey log, drag-to-place/move, connect/delete modes, zoom/fit, all persisted to campaign.narrative in localStorage.

commits.message: feat(dndm): Phase 8 — character sheet, party bar, bestiary/spellbook advanced filters, Oracle Ask buttons
commits.why: Four major Grimoire UX upgrades: full 5e character sheet per PC (ability scores, saves, skills with proficiency cycling, spell slots per level, features/equipment/personality/backstory/alignment/languages, live localStorage persistence); party status bar sticky strip visible during session mode showing HP bars, conditions, and KO state with click-to-jump; bestiary advanced filters (CR range, 14 creature type pills, 6 size pills, compound-stacking with name search); spellbook advanced filters (level 0-9 pills, 8 school pills, concentration/ritual toggles, 8 class pills); Oracle Ask buttons on monster stat blocks and spell detail panels that pre-fill contextual questions.

commits.message: feat(dndm): Phase 6 — Atlas (fog of war), Soundscape, Player Screen
commits.why: Phase 6 of Grimoire: Atlas with pan/zoom viewport, canvas fog-of-war grid (paint-to-reveal), DM markers, map upload/delete, and BroadcastChannel player screen push. Soundscape with scene preset cards, HTMLAudio playback, file upload via createObjectURL, volume/loop controls. Player Screen (?view=player) receives map+fog state and renders fullscreen canvas.

commits.message: fix(dndm): welcome screen overlay blocked all interaction after dismiss / feat(dndm): Phase 7 — Oracle (nano-gpt streaming rules assistant)
commits.why: Root cause of 'nothing is clickable' was .welcome { display: flex } outranking the browser UA [hidden] rule, keeping the fixed overlay alive after dismissal — fixed with .welcome[hidden] { display: none !important }. Also ships Phase 7: Oracle streaming chat with inline markdown renderer, nano-gpt API key persistence, and Phase 8 keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+B/P/D/O, N for next turn).

commits.message: fix(dndm): campaign init safety — deep-merge guard, remove duplicate tab listener
commits.why: Phase 5 was non-interactive after deploy: Object.assign in campaignLoad() was corrupting campaign.lore when stale localStorage data predated the lore sub-object, causing a boot-time TypeError; also removed a duplicate .panel__tab listener from before Phase 5 that fired redundantly.

commits.message: feat(dndm): Phase 5 — Campaign data layer, Sessions log, NPCs, Lore Wiki
commits.why: Phase 5 of DM MATRIX: full Campaign overview (editable name/setting/banner/scratch pad), NPC tracker (add/edit/remove with role, location, relationship, alive state), Lore Wiki (tabbed locations/factions/timeline entries), Session log (add/edit/remove sessions with XP and summary), all wired to localStorage persistence and export/import campaign JSON.

commits.message: redesign(hub): sovereign nodes — instrument-panel tiles (final approved)
commits.why: Sixth and final sovereign nodes redesign — instrument-panel square tiles (148px, centred icon, animated status dot, mono typography) approved by trap after five rejected attempts; this design is visually distinct from Station (cyan HUD bars) and Transmissions (warm bloom cards).

commits.message: redesign(hub): sovereign nodes — infrastructure rack cards replacing terminal block
commits.why: Fourth sovereign nodes redesign attempt (rack cards with corner brackets) — still rejected by trap; documented here as part of the iterative redesign history before final instrument-tile approach.

commits.message: redesign(hub): three distinct section treatments — terminal, HUD scan, broadcast cards
commits.why: Third sovereign nodes attempt alongside Station (cyan HUD bars) and Transmissions (warm bloom cards) — terminal block rejected; Station and Transmissions approved from this commit onward.

commits.message: redesign(hub): node-tile component — glass surface, accent bar, hover lift for sovereign/station/trap sections
commits.why: First and second sovereign nodes redesign attempts (plain list, glass tiles) — rejected as too generic; iterative redesign in search of a unique visual identity.

commits.message: feat(dndm): Phase 4 — Party Tracker + Combat Initiative Tracker
commits.why: Phase 4 of DM MATRIX: fully functional Party Tracker (PC cards with HP bar, heal/damage controls, death saves, condition picker, sessionStorage persistence) and Combat Tracker (initiative order, NEXT TURN, per-combatant HP edit, dead state, Send-Party-to-Combat from party module) — the two most critical session-time tools the DM partner will use.

commits.message: feat(hub): sovereign nodes compact list, statstation+trap sections, social strip in footer, void/new editor
commits.scope: index.html, glass/script/modules/core.js, glass/script/modules/ui.js, glass/style/modules/components.css, glass/style/modules/layout.css, void/new/index.html
commits.why: Sovereign nodes restyled as compact node-list rows (matching drone fleet footprint); statstation.json and trap.json now render dedicated sections on the home page; radial social cluster removed, replaced with flat icon strip in footer; void/new/index.html — local-only entry editor with live markdown preview, ID generator, and JSON fragment export for easy void.json authoring.

commits.message: feat(void): consolidate 86 .md files into void.json, add marked+DOMPurify rendering, markdown prose CSS
commits.why: All 86 .md source files consolidated into a single authoritative void.json (48 entries after dedup), with marked.js + DOMPurify replacing the hand-rolled toProse() — enabling real markdown rendering with headings, blockquotes, bold, code, lists, and a custom VOID-sig extension; comprehensive markdown prose CSS added matching the hub's design vocabulary.

commits.message: feat(void): complete redesign — data-driven feed, spotlight overlay, eerie depth layers
commits.why: Void page needed a complete ground-up redesign to match the intended aesthetic: deep-water dark, eerie unsettling texture, data-driven from void.json, spotlight overlay for full entries, no glass/ dependency.

commits.message: refactor(structure): lunchbox pass — declutter root, unify css loading, tuck drafts
commits.why: Root was cluttered with loose images, scratch data in the production data layer, and AI context files scattered across directories — lunchbox pass tightened everything into its logical home.

commits.message: refactor(paths): replace all relative traversals with absolute root paths
commits.scope: glass/script/modules/core.js, llm.js, guide/index.html, playground/index.html, playground/script/main.js, radar/index.html, status/index.html, status/logs/index.html, underdark/index.html, underdark/script/modules/*
commits.why: All relative path traversals (../../glass/, ../glass/, ../../../../glass/) replaced with absolute root paths (/glass/...) — eliminates brittle depth-sensitive strings that break silently when files are moved, and makes every cross-app reference self-documenting at a glance.

commits.message: refactor(repo): restructure — underdark/ top-level, art/assets, radar/assets, remove empty dirs
commits.scope: entire repo — directory structure, all cross-references updated
commits.why: playground/characters was buried 2 levels deep with brittle ../../../../glass/ paths; art and radar each had a confusing inner 'glass/' folder despite being fully self-contained; shared/ and universe/ were empty stubs — restructured for clarity, shorter paths, and future app additions under playground/.

commits.message: fix(underdark): terminal header 50px→36px, arena.css?v=10 + sidebar.css?v=2 cache-bust
commits.why: Two persistent visual gaps: right sidebar header was 50px tall (matched arena header height but showed almost nothing), and thread top padding still appearing large because browsers were serving cached arena.css despite code change. Terminal header shrunk to 36px; all CSS modules now have explicit version query strings.

commits.message: feat(underdark): image IDB persist fix, wallhaven in profile, terminal visual redesign — ui.js v114
commits.scope: playground/characters — ui.js, index.html, terminal.css, style.css, main.js
commits.why: Three user-reported issues: (1) images still vanished on reload — the placeholder→real swap path bypassed _injectImageMessage and never called addMessage; (2) Wallhaven gallery was only accessible via header overflow menu — added to Terminal Profile tab; (3) Terminal sidebar felt visually flat — redesigned with glowing tabs, avatar pulse ring, gradient name, accent edge dividers, shimmer action buttons.

commits.message: fix(underdark): move profile btn into sidebar + bust CSS cache for thread padding — ui.js v113
commits.scope: playground/characters — index.html, ui.js, utilities.css, style.css, arena.css, main.js
commits.why: Profile button was fixed bottom-left floating over the UI — moved inside sidebar so it hides when sidebar collapses. arena.css v9 + style.css v79 cache-bust ensures previously committed thread padding reduction (40px→12px) actually loads in browser.

commits.message: fix(underdark): reduce thread top padding from 40px to 12px — arena.css
commits.why: Thread top padding was var(--space-lg)=40px, creating a large dead zone before the first message. Reduced to var(--space-sm)=12px.

commits.message: fix(underdark): thread clip, persona undefined, create char, scenario timeout, image IDB persist, remove uplink node — ui.js v112
commits.why: User-reported: thread top clipped by overflow:hidden stacking context; persona fields showed literal 'undefined'; no visible Create Character entry in roster panel; scenario finder spun forever on network hang; injected images lost on page reload; UPLINK floating button redundant since profile flyout has the same link.

commits.message: fix(underdark): pass charMeta to buildAvatarHtml in appendMessage — ui.js v111
commits.why: Bot messages rendered with an empty avatar circle when avatar_path was an unresolvable IDB ref (getAvatarUrlSync returns null for async IDB refs). buildAvatarHtml falls back to emoji when avatar is null but needs charMeta to derive a character-appropriate emoji — previously null was passed, causing the default 👤 to render incorrectly in some conditions.

commits.message: feat(underdark): image studio generation history strip — ui.js v110
commits.why: Previously, generating a second image lost the first — no way to compare or restore without scrolling the thread. Added _studioHistory[] (session-local, max 20), pushed on each successful generation; studio canvas now shows a horizontal thumbnail strip (This session) above the preview so prior generations remain accessible and can be restored with one click.

commits.message: feat(underdark): video gallery — persist generated videos, gallery modal, profile strip — ui.js v109
commits.why: Generated videos disappeared after modal close — they were injected into the thread but never persisted anywhere retrievable. Added videoGallery[] to ensureGalleryStore; vg-inject and new vg-save-gallery button both push to it; profile tab now shows a video-strip (last 4 tiles) + View Video Gallery button that opens a full modal with grid + inline player view.

commits.message: feat(underdark): taggable gallery with per-image tag chips + filter bar — ui.js v108
commits.why: Gallery images had no metadata — flat ref array with no tagging, captioning, or filtering. Added galleryMeta parallel store (ref → {tags[]}) to ensureGalleryStore; gallery modal now renders tag chips per item, inline tag-add input, tag-remove click, and a filter bar above the grid showing all active tags.

commits.message: feat(underdark): thread lore pins + relationship map canvas — ui.js v107
commits.scope: playground/characters — ui.js, llm-engine.js, index.html, thread-config.css, scene-codex.css, style.css, main.js
commits.why: Two long-deferred Batch E features: (1) pinnedLoreBookIds in threadConfig was a dead schema field — now surfaced in Thread Config modal as a checklist of active lorebooks, and honoured in llm-engine.js buildPayload by marking pinned book entries alwaysOn before scanLorebooks runs; (2) groupConfig.relationships data had no visual — now a canvas relationship map overlay in the Scene Codex footer, circular layout, draws edges with relationship label text and avatar/initials nodes.

commits.message: feat(underdark): auto-save + Ctrl+K command palette — ui.js v106
commits.scope: playground/characters — ui.js, index.html, main.js, controls.css, style.css
commits.why: Auto-save protects long sessions from browser cache loss by silently writing a full instance JSON to localStorage every N turns (default 20, configurable, 0=off) with a download button in settings. Ctrl+K command palette gives power-user fuzzy-search access to all 24 actions from a single overlay, replacing the need to remember single-key shortcuts.

commits.message: feat(underdark): auto entrance on character join + autoEntrance flag — ui.js v105
commits.why: Phase 6 item 10 — when a character is added to a live session (history.length > 0), auto-fire an Overlord entrance narration to give the scene a cinematic arrival moment, rather than silently inserting the character; can be toggled off via the new autoEntrance narrative flag.

commits.message: feat(underdark): Scene Codex redesign v2 — cast strip with star/favorite, 4-col layout, footer stats, richer meters — ui.js v104
commits.scope: playground/characters — ui.js, index.html, scene-codex.css, style.css, main.js
commits.why: User requested a visual redesign of the Scene Codex god-view overlay with character starring/favoriting — the old 3-col layout was dense and lacking identity; new design adds a horizontal cast strip at the top with per-character star buttons, splits into 4 columns (Scene State / Narrative Controls / Scene Digest / Oracle), moves session stats to a clean footer strip, and redesigns all meters with directional color names and glow.

commits.message: feat(underdark): thread activity log — per-thread event feed with inline editing — ui.js v103
commits.scope: playground/characters — ui.js, state.js, index.html, thread-config.css, main.js
commits.why: User asked for a per-thread event log that auto-captures message summaries, scene transitions, and character events — all editable inline — so they have a running record of what happened in each thread without having to scroll the full message history.

commits.message: feat(underdark): redesign Thread Settings modal — full tc-* system — ui.js v102
commits.scope: playground/characters — ui.js, index.html, main.js, style/thread-config.css (new), style/style.css
commits.why: Thread Settings modal needed a complete visual overhaul — the old flat tab layout with generic checkbox toggles, plain textarea fields, and basic quick pills didn't match the design quality of the rest of the app; the new design introduces a dedicated CSS namespace (tc-*), left-side icon nav, section cards, custom toggle switches, energy scale for sexual energy, icon-chip tone selector, and pacing grid.

commits.message: feat(underdark): profile flyout, settings restructure, expanded gen params, suffix completion — ui.js v101
commits.scope: playground/characters — ui.js, index.html, main.js, style/utilities.css, style/settings-modal.css, data/cards/*
commits.why: Profile flyout replaces the settings header button with a NanoGPT-style avatar button (bottom-left, next to Uplink) that reveals a menu linking to Settings, My Persona, Backup, and Uplink Home; Interface tab removed from Settings (Arena Background lives in Chat Tint overlay); Generation expanded with Typical P, TFS-Z, Epsilon/ETA cutoffs, Seed control with roll+lock; Wallhaven API key surfaced in Neural panel; Thread Settings moved into More overflow dropdown; final 12 character system_prompt_suffix fields completed (rebecca.json JSON syntax error fixed first).

commits.message: feat(underdark): Batch C — Living World stream + post-from-thread — ui.js v98
commits.why: Batch C: Living World stream mode lets characters post autonomously on a configurable timer (AI-generated captions via fetchCompletion, typewriter animation on inject, pause/resume/speed control); post-from-thread adds an action button to bot messages that AI-generates an in-character caption and saves a local feed post — both features turn the social feed from static to live.

commits.message: refactor(underdark): Batch A+B — ctx object refactor + right sidebar Profile/Codex/Config tabs — ui.js v97
commits.scope: playground/characters — ui.js, index.html, main.js, terminal.css, style.css
commits.why: Batch A: introduced ctx object centralising all cross-section mutable state (galleryCharId, feedMode, permanentFeedPosts, activeSidebarTab, studioPresets/Wired/CharId/Model/DataUrl, oracleHistory/Streaming, $chatArena/$feedArena) — architectural foundation for future module splits. Batch B: renamed right sidebar tabs from Character/World/Settings to Profile/Codex/Config with icons, aligning labels with actual content semantics (Profile=character card, Codex=world+lorebooks, Config=settings+persona).

commits.message: feat(underdark): hard reset button in continuity config — wipe all data with optional image preservation — ui.js v96
commits.why: User requested a subtle factory reset button in continuity config that wipes all API keys, localStorage, sessionStorage, and IDB — prompting whether to preserve generated images — so the app can be reset to a clean first-run state without manually clearing browser storage. Three-option dialog (Keep Images / Wipe Everything / Cancel) implemented inline without confirm() to allow the third cancel path.

commits.message: feat(underdark): quality pass — thread config gear, memory compression, codex-meters.js module — ui.js v92
commits.scope: playground/characters — ui.js, llm-engine.js, index.html, arena.css, controls.css, style.css, main.js, new codex-meters.js
commits.why: Quality pass targeting three Priority 1 gaps: (1) Thread Config gear — #btn-thread-config in arena header opens modal with Core tab (scenario, model, temp, maxOutput, player name/persona, auto-attach lorebooks) and Tone tab (all narrativeTone fields, quick pills); gear dot-badge activates when any overrides are set; saved to chat.threadConfig, takes effect on next response. (2) Memory compression summaries — summarizeDroppedMessages() in llm-engine.js fires a 300-token background LLM call on dropped messages, caches per chatId+horizonMsgId in sessionStorage, patches summary into horizon divider when ready; .context-horizon__summary block styled in amber mono. (3) codex-meters.js module extraction — _applyStatusTags, _setMeterDOM, _keywordMeterScore, _llmScoreMeters, updateCodexMeters moved from ui.js closure into a proper ES module with init() dependency injection pattern; ui.js retains shim functions for backward-compatible call sites.

commits.message: feat(underdark): batch B — player identity, scene ledger, digest, horizon, AI quick replies, variants, scene markers, char targeting, prompt inspector — ui.js v91
commits.scope: playground/characters — ui.js, llm-engine.js, state.js, index.html, arena.css, scene-codex.css, controls.css, style.css, main.js
commits.why: Batch B — 11 features implementing the next layer of immersion and information architecture: (6) Player bubble lighter-variant UFR rendering — rp-* classes at 78% intensity for user messages; monogram initial avatar for players; (7) Scene markers — sceneBreak field on message schema, scene-divider element injected before each Overlord block; (8) Message variants — retry appends old content to variants[], chevron navigator with counter rendered below message; (9) Structured codex digest — live char rows with avatar/name/last-line, Overlord mode badge, scene number badge; (11) Auto transition every N=8 turns — fires _fireOverlord transition if no Overlord block appeared in last N messages; (12) Memory horizon UI — context cut divider with lock icon; out-of-context messages faded to 35% + sepia; (13) Memory depth 20; (14) Scene ledger — 4 structured codex slots injected into buildOverlordContext; (15) Char-targeted directives — group mode shows char picker on inject buttons; (16) AI contextual quick replies — background LLM generates 4 reply starters per turn, shown as warm-gold chips; (18) Structured prompt inspector — payload messages parsed into labelled details with role badge + token count; (19) Scene number tracking via _countScenes().

commits.message: feat(underdark): foundation batch — rp-action layer, meter feedback, cue card enrichment, narrative tone in DMs — ui.js v90
commits.scope: playground/characters — ui.js, llm-engine.js, arena.css, index.html, style.css, main.js
commits.why: Five tightly-coupled improvements to the LLM information architecture: (1) rp-action visual layer — marked.js converts *asterisk* to <em>, post-sanitize pass converts those to <span class=rp-action> (blue-silver glow); UFR directive expanded with Physical action as 4th explicit layer with examples; narration regex updated from <em>-exclusion to rp-action-exclusion; (2) STATUS tag feedback loop — bot responses containing [TENSION X%] etc. directly update codex meter DOM via _applyStatusTags(), closing the loop between LLM output and the meters display; (3) LLM-scored meters — hybrid system: keyword heuristic runs immediately after each turn, background fetchCompletion every 3 turns returns {tension,intimacy,danger} JSON and overrides the keyword estimate, sessionStorage caches per-history-length to avoid re-scoring; (4) Enriched group cue card — post-history anchor now includes last line from each other character + live meter state string, giving the LLM full situational awareness at the moment it writes; (5) Narrative tone in DM threads — tone fields (sexual energy, tone tags, amplify, avoid, pacing) moved from Group-only tab to Generation tab so DM threads can configure tone; llm-engine.js injects [NARRATIVE TONE DIRECTIVE] system message for DM threads when tone is set; tone collected once in thread setup and mirrored into gc for group threads.

commits.message: feat(underdark): player identity sheet, Overlord context awareness, entrance/whisper/irony modes — ui.js v89
commits.scope: playground/characters — ui.js, llm-engine.js, index.html, char-editor.css, terminal.css, main.js; status/logs/logs.json
commits.why: Three interconnected improvements to how information flows between the system and the LLM: (1) Player identity — replaced bare 'About User' text blob with a structured player sheet (appearance, mood, role, status) injected via buildPlayerBlock; characters now know who they're interacting with as a fully-realised person; (2) Overlord context awareness — _fireOverlord now reads live meter values, player fields, and scenario and passes all of it into a richly structured Overlord system prompt via buildOverlordContext export; Overlord narration is now scene-specific not generic; (3) UFR moved to top of system prompt so format rules are read before character identity (harder to override by creative content); responseFormat block in buildAIDirectivesBlock suppressed to prevent conflict with UFR markup; (4) Three new Overlord modes: 'entrance' (emerald), 'whisper' (silver/italic), 'irony' (amber-orange) — each with codex admin buttons and distinct CSS palette.

commits.message: feat(underdark): force-trigger, character isolation, UFR format system, Overlord expansion — ui.js v88
commits.scope: playground/characters — ui.js, llm-engine.js, state.js, main.js, index.html, arena.css, char-editor.css, style.css; status/logs/logs.json
commits.why: Four-pillar overhaul requested by trap: (1) Force-trigger — zap buttons on group character pills + overflow 'Force Response' item let the user manually provoke any character to respond without sending a user message; (2) Character isolation — three-layer bleed prevention: GROUP ISOLATION block in system prompt, full history attribution prefixing ALL messages with speaker name in group mode, and a post-history 'It is now X's turn' anchor; (3) UFR (Underdark Format Ruleset) — a universal format directive injected into every system prompt specifying double-quote speech, asterisk action, tilde thought, plain prose narration, and [STATUS] bracket tags; tilde thought rendering added to renderMarkdown; (4) Overlord expansion — _fireOverlord helper centralises all Overlord LLM calls, four new modes (transition/reaction/moment/recap) with distinct CSS palettes, five codex admin buttons wired via _codexAdminBtn boilerplate-eliminator, overlordMode persisted to message history and restored on renderFullHistory.

commits.message: fix(underdark): guard spark/ideas buttons with API key check; log picker errors
commits.why: showPickerModal's .catch() silently swallowed all fetchCompletion errors — when no API key was set the modal showed 'Generation failed' with no actionable feedback. Added getApiKey() guard at the top of the three spark/ideas handlers (.ts-spark-field-btn, .ts-gen-ideas-btn, _tsSparkSceneIdeas) to surface the failure as a toast instead. Also added console.warn to the .catch() so errors appear in DevTools.

commits.scope: playground/characters — state.js, storage.js, ui.js, index.html, char-editor.css, main.js; shared-utils.js; status/logs/logs.json
commits.why: Full instance backup: storage.js gained idbGetAllEntries/idbSetBulk for bulk IDB cursor walk; state.js exports exportFullInstance (full dump to underdark_full_v1 JSON) and importFullInstance (full restore with IDB bulk write + state sanitize + confirm guard). LLM response parsing centralised: shared-utils.js gained parseLLMArray/parseLLMJson/parseLLMLines — 7 inline ad-hoc patterns in ui.js replaced. Settings panel gained Full Backup section. Import All button has subtle red tint to signal destructive action.

commits.message: fix(underdark): maxTokens→max_tokens in all fetchCompletion calls; model fallback; scroll after image swap
commits.why: Phase 2 fetchCompletion calls used camelCase maxTokens which the OpenAI-compatible API ignores — silent failure meant the model ran uncapped on scenario wizard, overlord auto-fire, recap, and force-overlord calls. Also added || 'deepseek-r1' fallback to 5 model fields that were undefined before user selected a model, and a scroll after the placeholder→real image swap.

commits.message: feat(underdark): phase 2 — emoji avatars, image placeholders, scenario AI wizard, codex admin, overlord auto-fire, continuity rules
commits.scope: playground/characters — ui.js, index.html, char-editor.css, main.js; data/scenario-index.json; status/logs/logs.json
commits.why: Large multi-feature pass requested by trap: continuity names enforced with ♾️ prefix and made permanent (no delete); threads also non-deletable (renamed only); group chats get emoji avatar picker; characters without URLs get thematic emoji placeholders; snapshot image gen now shows animated progress placeholder until image completes; redo button on images opens 5-question style picker to refine next gen; scenario AI recommendation wizard uses compact index JSON to keep LLM context lean; codex god panel gains Redirect/Tension/Reveal/Vulnerable inject buttons plus Admin section (Re-describe, Recap, Reset Meters, Snapshot); group chats auto-fire Overlord scene-setting when history exists but no block is present; pending_work section added to logs.json for cross-session tracking.

commits.message: fix(underdark): resolve 11 bugs from codebase audit
commits.why: Comprehensive bug audit found 3 critical, 5 major, and 4 minor issues — IDB/localStorage race condition on image save, detached group chat loops that survived turn boundaries, stale streaming closure writing responses to wrong chats, lucideRefresh re-initialising every icon on the page on every call (~98 times per session), listener leaks on repeated modal opens, orphan placeholder DOM nodes on stream failure, and > character encoding breaking character card display.

commits.message: style(overlord): amber/gold palette, slow pulse animation
commits.why: User requested a completely unique color palette for the Overlord block distinct from the site's violet accent — switched to amber/gold (#E8A830, #C8780A, #7A4A06) with a 4s ease-in-out box-shadow pulse to give the narrator presence without flickering.

commits.message: feat(ui): Overlord block — cinematic narrator band replacing system message bubble
commits.why: System/scene-intro messages were rendering as plain chat bubbles — indistinguishable from bot messages. Overlord block gives narrator injections a full-width cinematic identity: scan-line texture, top glow bar, EVE-like rails with OVERLORD label, fade-in animation, distinct typography.

commits.message: feat(ui): choice picker modal, spark/suggest via 3-option AI picker, fix image download, improve scene-intro style
commits.why: Spark/suggest/amplify/avoid buttons were filling directly with no visibility into what the AI generated — rewritten to show a 3-option choice picker (with free-text Other fallback); image download used raw data URL which browsers sometimes treat as file-picker; scene-intro lacked visual weight for the opening scene of a group roleplay.

commits.message: fix: missing comma in index.json after Lady Jessica avatar_path update
commits.why: Previous session updated Lady Jessica's avatar_path but dropped the trailing comma, producing invalid JSON — loadManifest() silently swallowed the parse error so all manifest characters failed to load on both local and production.

commits.message: feat(characters): narrative tone config, per-field spark buttons, group profile panel, last-speaker portrait, gallery picker, thread model override fix
commits.scope: playground/characters — ui.js, llm-engine.js, state.js, index.html, arena.css, char-editor.css
commits.why: Phase 2+3 of group chat overhaul: narrative tone system (sexual energy, tone tags, amplify/avoid/pacing) injected into every group LLM response; per-field AI spark buttons on all context questions and relationship pairs; Spark ideas + Generate for opening scene; gallery save in groups now shows a char picker instead of defaulting to activeBotId; pill clicks in group chats no longer switch the profile panel; last-speaker portrait updates the group background; group right panel shows multi-member overview + narrative tone summary; thread model override now wins over per-character modelOverride so setting deepseek-v3.2 on a thread actually uses v3.2.

commits.message: fix(ui): build all speech/thought regexes via RegExp constructor with \uXXXX escapes
commits.why: VSCode silently auto-curled straight " into typographic " in regex literals — RegExp constructor with \uXXXX is the only safe way to include quote chars.

commits.message: refactor(ui): rewrite renderMarkdown — post-DOMPurify injection, no token system
commits.why: Pre-tokenise approach had three failure points (editor curling, DOMPurify guillemet encoding, esc() double-encoding) — post-DOMPurify text-node injection eliminates all of them.

commits.message: debug(ui): decode DOMPurify-encoded guillemets before rpFlush; add token log
commits.why: DOMPurify entity-encodes « (U+00AB) to « in text nodes — needed decode pass to restore placeholders before flush regex could match them.

commits.message: fix(ui): use RegExp constructor for speech quote detection — editor was auto-curling literals
commits.why: Speech detection regex with literal " chars never matched because VS Code converted them to curly quotes on save.

commits.message: fix(css): version-bust arena, scene-codex, responsive imports to clear browser cache
commits.why: arena.css + scene-codex.css changes were committed but browsers were serving stale cached @import versions — query param bumps force re-fetch.

commits.message: fix(arena): remove scene-codex.css bubble+rp overrides; fix curly-quote speech detection
commits.why: scene-codex.css had stale .message--bot/.message--user .message__bubble rules loaded after arena.css — killing dark glass gradient and gold speech colour. Speech regex only matched straight quotes but LLMs output curly/smart quotes; unified to [""…""] char class.

commits.message: feat(arena): chat bubble overhaul — RP layers, entry animations, streaming indicator
commits.why: Chat message rendering had flat white text with no visual hierarchy — every narrative layer (narration, action, speech, thought, system tags) looked identical. Complete overhaul: bot bubbles now deep dark glass with left accent bar + hover reveal gradient; user bubbles warm amber gradient; slide-in animations per side; streaming class with pulsing left bar; RP text layers given distinct colours/glows (gold speech, violet thought, dim lavender narration, monospace status tags); [STATUS value] bracket patterns tokenised to rp-tag badges; rp-narration class auto-applied to pure narration paragraphs.

commits.message: refactor(image-engine): intelligent prompt builder + Describe Scene feature
commits.why: buildImagePrompt was dumping raw char editor prose directly into image prompts — fields like distinctiveFeatures and scarsMarks contain narrative text with parentheticals and semicolons that image models treat as noise. Lorebook entries (world lore) were also being injected, producing completely irrelevant tokens like 'The spice melange is the most valuable substance...' in Jinx's prompt. Replaced with _cleanVisualField() keyword extractor, removed lore/scenario/history injection from the static builder, rewrote the LLM path with a structured char sheet format, and added the Describe Scene UI with charWeight/sceneWeight sliders.

commits.message: fix(responsive): 100dvh + safe-area overhaul; wallhaven grid scroll + purity borders
commits.why: responsive.css complete rewrite: iOS 100vh clips content under Dynamic Island/home bar — replaced with 100dvh + -webkit-fill-available, all safe-area-insets absorbed once on .underdark-layout with no double-application, full device cascade from iPad to iPhone SE. wallhaven.css: Load More was squishing tiles because .wh-grid had height:100% inside overflow:hidden parent — fixed by moving scroll ownership to .wh-grid-area and using min-height on the grid; purity-coded glow-pulse border animations added (sfw=green, sketchy=amber, nsfw=red, staggered 4-5s periods).

commits.message: feat: Wallhaven arena full UX overhaul — god-tier tiles, lightbox, shimmer, mobile nav fix, auto-fetch
commits.why: Wallhaven was functional but flat — tiles had no shimmer skeleton, the lightbox showed a blank src flash instead of a blurred thumbnail placeholder, the mobile nav was positioned between header and content instead of at the bottom, the char-name-changed check was always false (WH.charName already overwritten before comparison), and opening without a character left an empty state instead of auto-fetching top results. Full CSS pass: richer hover animations, touch-device always-visible overlays, masonry via CSS columns, keyboard shortcut strip in lightbox and empty state, improved spacing and depth throughout.

Now feed reads only from feed.json (permanent) and state.socialData.localPosts (local). addToGallery auto-creates a localPost mirror so every generated/uploaded image still appears in the feed. Gallery delete removes the matching localPost. All post-count displays updated to getAllFeedPosts. Gallery array is now solely for lightbox/strip.

commits.message: feat: persistent social feed — feed.json, compose modal, local/permanent post merge, export
commits.why: Social feed was purely ephemeral — all posts (gallery images, generated art) lived only in localStorage with no path to permanent visibility across instances. Added data/feed.json as the git-committed post store (git push = visible everywhere), compose modal for in-app post creation as any character (text or image URL), getAllFeedPosts() merging gallery IDB images + permanent feed.json + local composed posts, loadFeedJson() parallel-fetched at startup, export-session-feed download button to extract local posts as a ready-to-merge JSON fragment. Post IDs replace index-based keys throughout for stable comment and like state.

commits.message: feat: immersive RP UX pass — animated streaming indicator, rich QR groups, speech/thought CSS, directional message animations
commits.why: The roleplay UI lacked visual feedback during streaming, quick-reply prompts were generic and flat, text rendering made no visual distinction between dialogue/thought/action, and message entrance animations were identical for user vs bot. Comprehensive UX pass: dynamic input placeholder reflecting active character, expanded QR bar with 4 themed groups × 4 evocative entries, animated streaming dots + character name pulse in bot-label, `.rp-speech` amber glow + `.rp-thought` violet shimmer + `em` muted silver layer in CSS, and differentiated spring-eased slide-in animations per message origin.

commits.message: fix: avatar resolution for gallery-promoted images; cache invalidation on avatar change
commits.why: When a gallery image (stored as idb:img: ref) was set as a character's avatar, getAvatarUrl would call resolveCharAvatar which looked in the wrong IDB store (avatar:<charId> vs img:<blobId>), returning null and showing a broken avatar. Also, the avatar cache was keyed only by charId and never invalidated on change, so newly set avatars continued showing the old image until page reload. Fixed getAvatarUrl to detect idb:img: refs and route to resolveImageUrl; changed cache structure to key on the ref value; added cache busting in both avatar-set paths.

commits.message: fix: resolve idb refs in social/hot feed; auto-refresh feed on image generation
commits.why: _renderPostList passed raw idb:img: refs directly into innerHTML as without resolving them via IndexedDB — generated images appeared broken in the social feed and hot feed. Added async resolution pass before rendering. Also wired social/hot feed live refresh after every image generation path so new images appear immediately without requiring the user to navigate away and back.
