sessions.summary: Two bugs fixed in Grimoire Story canvas. (1) Node right-click context menu never appeared: root cause was JavaScript closure scope — storyNodeCtxShow/Hide were defined inside initStory() but called from storyCreateNodeEl which is at the outer DOMContentLoaded scope; fix was to move both functions and the $nodeCtxMenu variable to outer scope. (2) Crow's foot marker heads never visually rendered after being changed in the edge popup: root cause was defs.innerHTML += on a detached SVG element — each += re-parses the accumulated string as HTML (not SVG namespace), losing marker functionality; fix was to build the full defs string in a JS variable and do one $svg.innerHTML = '<defs>...' assignment so the browser parser sees all markers in SVG namespace context.
decisions: storyNodeCtxShow/Hide moved to top-level scope alongside storyRenderEdges, storyEdgePopupShow etc — all story helpers that are called from storyCreateNodeEl must live at outer scope
$nodeCtxMenu declared as null at outer scope alongside other _story* vars, assigned inside initStory() after DOM is ready
SVG defs injection changed from defs.innerHTML += (loop) to $svg.innerHTML = '<defs>' + fullString + '</defs>' (single write) — this is the only safe way to inject SVG marker markup from JS strings
rules_reinforced: Functions called from outer-scope handlers must not be defined in inner scopes — storyCreateNodeEl is at DOMContentLoaded level, so its callees must be too
Never use innerHTML += on SVG/MathML elements — build the full string and assign once

sessions.summary: Grimoire continuation (resumed from compaction after context limit). Added node right-click context menu: right-clicking any story canvas node now shows a 4-item quick-action menu — Edit in Composer, Set as Now Playing (disabled if already active), Add Branch (creates child node + edge), and Delete Node (in danger style, separated by a visual divider). The node contextmenu event prevents canvas contextmenu propagation; both menus dismiss on Escape, outside-click, or when a menu item is actioned. CSS extended with .story-ctx-menu__item--danger, --disabled, and .story-ctx-menu__sep.
decisions: Node ctx menu reuses the same .story-ctx-menu BEM block with a --node modifier; no separate component needed
Set as Now Playing is shown but disabled (not hidden) when the node is already active — gives visual feedback that the action exists
Separator before Delete is a deliberate UX pattern — destructive actions need visual breathing room from non-destructive ones
e.stopPropagation() on node contextmenu prevents the canvas add-node menu from appearing simultaneously
rules_reinforced: Context menus should show disabled state rather than hide unavailable items — preserves discoverability
Destructive actions get a visual separator and danger color — never inline with safe actions

sessions.summary: Grimoire continuation session (resumed from compaction). Implemented campaign slot system: multi-campaign named saves with dndm_campaigns index array and per-slot dndm_campaign_<id> keys; auto-migration of legacy single-slot data to 'camp_legacy'. New Campaign replaced prompt() with Composer — a new 'new-campaign' mode that collects name, setting, hook, tone, and secrets upfront before creating the slot. Load Campaign modal lists all saved slots as clickable cards (name, setting, hook preview, date, delete button) plus Import from File (JSON) and Cancel. API key restored to Settings modal per user feedback. Story node desc/notes textareas converted to Composer triggers — clicking opens a 'story-node' Composer mode with full-height prose textareas; inline textareas now show preview value as readonly with cursor:pointer affordance and expand hint labels. storySyncEditor title and tags still update inline; Composer commits write back to the node object and sync inline fields. Save button label becomes 'Create Campaign' in new-campaign mode.
decisions: New Campaign uses Composer instead of prompt() — gathers full campaign context (name/setting/hook/tone/secrets) at creation time, not just a name
Load Campaign modal uses card-per-slot layout with delete-in-place; slot index stored separately from slot data for fast listing without parsing every campaign
Story node textareas use mousedown+preventDefault to intercept click before browser focuses the readonly field, preventing cursor flash
story-node Composer mode syncs inline editor panel values on save so sidebar stays consistent without a full re-render
API key was deliberately restored to Settings after initial removal — user confirmed they want it in both Settings and Oracle panel
rules_reinforced: prompt() is never acceptable in Grimoire — Composer is the answer for any text-heavy new-entity flow
readonly textarea + mousedown intercept is the correct pattern for 'click to expand' fields that show preview text
All slot index entries must store name/setting/hook/date for the Load modal to render without parsing per-campaign data

sessions.summary: Built the Grimoire Composer — a full-screen distraction-free writing overlay replacing all browser prompt() dialogs. The Composer has 4 modes: campaign-meta (name, setting, hook, tone guide, master secrets), npc (name, role, location, relationship, status dropdown, portrait URL, full notes textarea), lore (title + full body textarea with tab-aware hints), and scratch (full-height monospace scratchpad). Ctrl+S saves, Esc discards with dirty-check confirmation. Topbar shows mode label, word count, and 'saved' flash. Expanded campaign data model: hook/tone/secrets at campaign level; NPC gets location/relationship/status/portrait/notes. Oracle context now injects campaign premise, tone guide, secrets, and NPC roster so suggestions are scenario-aware. API key removed from Settings panel (still exists in the Story Oracle key row). Banner gets an 'Edit' button that opens campaign-meta Composer.
decisions: Full-screen fixed overlay (inset:0) with dark background — no modal frame, no backdrop click-to-close — forces intentional Save or Discard action
Serif font (Georgia) for prose textareas — satisfying to write in, distinct from the mono UI chrome
Dirty flag tracked on any input event — discard requires confirm() only when changes exist
Campaign secrets injected into Oracle system prompt as [DM ONLY] context — LLM sees the full picture for suggestions without ever showing players
NPC status uses colored dot (green/red/grey/amber/purple) in card view — immediate visual scan of who is alive/dead/missing
API key removed from Settings — it's already in the Oracle panel key row; duplicate was confusing
rules_reinforced: prompt() is never acceptable for multi-line content — Composer pattern is the answer for any text-heavy edit in this codebase
Full-screen overlays at z-index:12000 are safe in dndm/ (below no other layer except welcome screen at 9000)
Campaign data model should be expanded proactively when adding LLM features — richer data = better suggestions

sessions.summary: Major ECHOES gallery (/art/) overhaul across 6 commits. (1) Architecture: replaced CSS columns with CSS grid + fixed grid-auto-rows; orientation probed via Image() before DOM insertion so grid never reflows on pull. (2) Filtering: fixed broken filters — detention.json uses type:'digital'/'traditional' but filter was reading metadata.medium (always empty); fixed by reading a.type at normalization. New 4th filter 'nsfw' shows NSFW-tagged artifacts without veils. (3) NSFW support: metadata.nsfw boolean flag; frosted glass veil (backdrop-filter blur+saturate+brightness) with 'SENSITIVE' ribbon + click-to-reveal; opacity+scale dissolve on reveal (clip-path rejected — clipped by overflow:hidden). 4 DEE7A4 artifacts marked nsfw. (4) Grid stability: _gridColCursor tracks column position across batches; landscape cards cap at span 2 (never span 3); landscape in last 1-col slot promoted to span 3 full row to prevent orphaned cells. (5) Shift+click pull: executeFloodPull() loads all remaining at once (ABYSS tier divider). (6) Multi-select: shift+click card toggles selection in slide-in panel with thumbnails, IDs, clipboard copy. (7) Card borders: animated border-color breathe (purple→bright purple→amber) with nth-child offsets for out-of-phase pulsing, hover flares to amber. (8) Pull button: enlarged, sensual copy, animated orb with rotating conic gradient ring. (9) 23 artifacts removed from DBDBF9 collection. (10) Card reveal: blur+desaturate entrance with chromatic shimmer.
decisions: CSS grid with fixed grid-auto-rows preferred over CSS columns — columns rebalance on append causing reflow; grid with pre-probed spans is stable
NSFW veil uses opacity+scale dissolve (not clip-path) — clip-path is clipped by card's overflow:hidden
NSFW filter is a 4th filter button not a toggle — aligns with the existing filter pattern, removes need for a separate UI element
Landscape cards capped at span 2 max — span 3 was stretching low-res landscape thumbnails unacceptably
Landscape in last 1-col slot promoted to span 3 full row — avoids orphaned empty cells that dense flow cannot backfill without a following portrait
Shift+click reveal secret (flood pull) kept undocumented — surprise mechanic, not telegraphed
border-color animation (card-edge-breathe) used instead of box-shadow outline tricks — browsers interpolate border-color reliably; box-shadow at low opacity is imperceptible against dark backgrounds
Removed 23 artifacts from DBDBF9 collection at trap's explicit request
rules_reinforced: art/ is self-contained — never reference glass/ from art/assets/
NSFW veil cannot use clip-path — overflow:hidden on the card clips it; use opacity/transform instead
CSS columns rebalance on DOM append — use CSS grid for galleries where cards are added incrementally
Card borders at low box-shadow opacity are invisible on near-black backgrounds — use actual border property

sessions.summary: Fixed ECHOES gallery thumbnails forcing all images into a 3/4 portrait aspect ratio, making landscape images appear incorrectly cropped. Removed the fixed aspect-ratio from .art-card and changed .art-card__thumb to width:100%; height:auto so each card's height is determined by the image's natural dimensions. Removed the mobile aspect-ratio overrides (4/3 landscape + 3/4 portrait) that are no longer needed. The .art-card--portrait class logic in app.js remains harmlessly.
decisions: Natural image sizing (height:auto) preferred over a fixed aspect-ratio container — galleries with mixed orientations should respect each image's native proportions
Removed the mobile 4/3 → 3/4 portrait override since it was compensating for the now-removed fixed ratio
object-fit:cover retained for hover zoom scale (transform:scale) to clip correctly at the overflow:hidden boundary
rules_reinforced: CSS-only fix — no JS required, no data changes
art/ is self-contained, does not reference glass/

sessions.summary: Diagnosed and fixed a silent crash in the Neural Uplink playground that caused all message sends to fail without any feedback. The root cause was a missing `history: state.history` property in the `ctx` object passed to `Chat.sendMessage` — the function destructured `history` from ctx but ctx only contained `state`, so history was undefined and `history.push()` threw a TypeError silently caught by the generic error handler before any DOM feedback was shown. Fixed by adding `history: state.history` to the ctx definition in llm.js. Simultaneously redesigned playground/index.html to elevate the visual identity: scanline ambient overlay (.playground-page__scanlines with a scrolling repeating-linear-gradient), inline SVG back arrow replacing the lucide icon dependency in the topbar, improved eyebrow identity block with NEURAL UPLINK // SOVEREIGN INFERENCE and a sub-label variant, boot message copy updated.
decisions: history: state.history added directly to ctx at definition — cleaner than passing via callbacks, matches how the function signature expects it
Scanlines use a CSS repeating-linear-gradient at 4px pitch, 4% opacity, animated with background-position — atmospheric without adding texture noise
prefers-reduced-motion kills the scanline animation (background-position 0 0 static)
playground-page__layout gets z-index: 1 so it renders above the fixed scanline layer
Topbar back arrow uses inline SVG polyline instead of data-lucide to avoid the lucide init dependency before boot completes
rules_reinforced: Always verify that objects passed as ctx to imported functions contain ALL destructured keys — missing keys fail silently as undefined, not as missing-property errors
CSS atmospheric effects (scanlines, grain) belong on a fixed position overlay with pointer-events: none and z-index: 0 — content always z-index: 1 above

sessions.summary: Phase 9 polish pass on Grimoire. Added coarse-pointer media query for touch-target compliance (≥44px min-height on toolbar buttons, type-picker pills, Oracle buttons, NOW PLAYING strip actions). Extended prefers-reduced-motion block to cover STORY canvas node transitions, Oracle loading animation, NOW PLAYING strip transitions, HP bar fill transitions, and character sheet overlay fade. Minor CSS visual improvements: NOW PLAYING strip box-shadow, node title font-size confirmed at 0.8rem. Updated logs.json to record the manually-made e45f69d commit (apps.json live status for Playground, Underdark, Grimoire).
decisions: @media (pointer: coarse) preferred over touch-action for min-height enforcement — more targeted than screen width breakpoints, correctly targets iPad/phone without affecting desktop
story-tool min-height 44px only on coarse-pointer — desktop retains compact 0.22rem padding aesthetic
prefers-reduced-motion: story-node transition disabled, oracle __loading animation disabled, party-bar HP fill transition disabled
rules_reinforced: Touch target minimum 44px applies to all interactive elements — implement via pointer: coarse media query not viewport breakpoints
prefers-reduced-motion must cover all new feature animations each session — not just the welcome splash

sessions.summary: Built the STORY module — a full narrative decision tree builder for campaign planning and live session navigation. Pan/zoom SVG+HTML canvas with 6 node types (Scene gold, Decision crimson, Event bone, Revelation purple, Combat red, Wildcard teal), bezier curved edges with arrow markers, node drag-and-drop repositioning, connect mode (click-to-link), delete mode (click-to-remove), snap-to-grid, zoom/fit. Right panel has node editor (title/desc/notes/tags/status/monsters) and Oracle suggestions panel (streaming nano-gpt contextual suggestions + Expand Tree mode that generates 5 child nodes and adds them to canvas on click). During session mode a NOW PLAYING strip shows the active scene with Off-Script (spawns wildcard node) and Mark Done (auto-advances to next unplayed child). Journey log tracks the actual path taken with timestamps. All data stored in campaign.narrative sub-object.
decisions: Nodes are HTML divs (not canvas) for easy DOM interaction and CSS styling; edges are SVG beziers in a transparent SVG layer behind
Canvas uses CSS transform (translate + scale) on a single stage div — no virtualisation needed at DnD scale (50-200 nodes)
Node types have dedicated CSS variables: --node-scene/decision/event/reveal/combat/wild
Oracle Expand mode generates [TYPE] Title — desc formatted nodes and creates real canvas nodes on suggestion click
NOW PLAYING strip only visible during session mode AND when a current node is set
Off-Script creates a Wildcard child, marks parent as altered, logs to journey — zero friction for mid-session improvisation
Mark Done auto-advances if exactly 1 unplayed child; shows toast if 0 or multiple — DM chooses the branch
rules_reinforced: async functions work fine inside DOMContentLoaded — already used for Oracle
CSS flex height propagation: height: 0 + flex-grow OR explicit height needed for nested flex containers to fill parent

sessions.summary: Continuation session (resumed after context compaction). Implemented four major Grimoire features: full 5e character sheet overlay per PC with localStorage persistence (ability scores, saves, skills with dot-cycling proficiency, spell slots, textareas for features/equipment/personality/backstory, live bonus recomputation); party status bar visible during session mode (sticky HP strip with condition dots and KO skull, click-to-jump to Party module); advanced compound filters for Bestiary (CR range, 14 type pills, 6 size pills) and Spellbook (level 0-9, 8 schools, concentration/ritual toggles, 8 class pills); Oracle Ask buttons on stat blocks and spell panels that pre-fill contextual D&D questions. Also fixed missing --purple-hi CSS variable, added +char-modal ability score fields, and wired renderPartyBar() into beginSession/endSession/refreshPCCard.
decisions: party stored in localStorage (not sessionStorage) so character sheets survive tab close
Skill proficiency cycles none → proficient → expert via dot click (0→1→2→0)
Party bar only shown when session is active (state.sessionActive) AND party has members
Advanced filters stack — all active filters are AND-ed together
Oracle Ask buttons pre-fill the prompt field but don't auto-send — user reviews first
bfilt-pill components are self-contained with data-* attributes, no ID dependencies
rules_reinforced: renderPartyBar must be called any time HP, conditions, or session state changes
All new DOM containers must be in index.html; JS only renders into them
CSS variables referenced but not defined cause silent failures — always define in :root

sessions.summary: DM MATRIX Phase 5 deployed (62e8517) but immediately reported broken — nothing clickable or interactable. Root-cause investigation found two issues: (1) a duplicate .panel__tab event listener from pre-Phase-5 code still present at boot time, and (2) Object.assign in campaignLoad() was clobbering campaign.lore when stale localStorage data from a previous session predated the lore sub-object, causing a boot-time TypeError that silently killed all event binding. Fix committed as ba99cc1. Also caught up logs.json with three missing commits (c346b1f sovereign nodes approval, 62e8517 Phase 5, ba99cc1 fix).
decisions: Object.assign is unsafe for nested state hydration — must always deep-merge validate after the assign when sub-keys could be absent in older saved data.
Duplicate event listeners at top-level scope are a fragile pattern — each module rewrite must audit for orphaned listeners from prior code.
Sidebar icon-only display at 985px viewport is correct responsive behaviour (max-width: 1199px collapses to icon strip), not a bug.
rules_reinforced: After every localStorage hydration with Object.assign, explicitly validate and reinitialise any nested sub-keys that may be absent in stale saved data.
When adding a new section to app.js, grep for any existing event listeners targeting the same selectors before adding new ones.

sessions.summary: Sovereign nodes section on index.html went through five full redesign iterations this session before reaching an approved design. Final approved design: compact square instrument-panel tiles (.node-tile) — 148px wide, centred icon, service name, hosting software, domain/shortcut/source in a footer row, animated status dot top-right, magenta bloom lift on hover. All previous sovereign nodes CSS/JS fully replaced. Session ended with commit + logs update, then pivot to DM MATRIX Phase 5.
decisions: Rejected designs (in order): plain list rows, glass tiles, macOS terminal block, rack cards with corner brackets, deployment manifest strips with oversized index number — all rejected as 'too generic' or 'terrible'.

Final approved concept: instrument panel tiles — compact squares resembling a control panel, each node its own self-contained unit. Distinct from Station (cyan bars) and Transmissions (warm bloom cards).
logs.json update bundled with sovereign nodes commit rather than as a standalone commit per protocol.
Next priority confirmed: DM MATRIX Phase 5 (Campaign + Sessions + Bestiary + Spellbook).
rules_reinforced: Every section on the hub must have a genuinely unique visual grammar — no two sections can share the same component pattern.
Iterating on sovereign nodes five times confirmed: trap will reject anything that looks like a standard card, list row, or generic UI pattern — uniqueness is non-negotiable.
Compact footprint is a strong preference for infrastructure sections — tiles, not large cards.

sessions.summary: Two parallel workstreams: (1) DM MATRIX Phase 4 — built fully functional Party Tracker (PC cards with HP tracking, death saves, conditions, sessionStorage persistence) and Combat Tracker (initiative order, NEXT TURN, per-combatant HP edit, dead-state, send-party-to-combat shortcut). (2) Home page quick-tasks: sovereign nodes restyled as compact rows, statstation.json + trap.json wired up as new page sections, radial social cluster replaced with flat icon strip in footer, void/new/index.html editor built for authoring new void entries with live preview + JSON export.
decisions: Tasks 1–4 (moving dndm/radar/underdark into playground as subfolders) deferred — path restructuring would break all internal asset paths across those apps and requires careful testing; not safe to rush with limited credits.
Sovereign nodes now use a compact node-row list component (.node-list / .node-row) instead of the larger grid-primary cards — matches the footprint philosophy of the drone fleet.
Radial social cluster removed entirely; social links now live as a flat .social-strip in the footer nav, matching the minimal aesthetic.
statstation.json renders under a 'Station' section title; trap.json renders under 'Transmissions' — both use the same node-row component as sovereign nodes.

void/new/index.html is local-only (noindex), two-pane editor with live markdown preview, auto-generated ID, word counter, and a JSON fragment export overlay — user pastes the fragment into void.json manually.
Party state persists in sessionStorage (not localStorage) — survives page refresh but clears on tab close, keeping the tracker ephemeral per-session.
rules_reinforced: Zero-build, no framework, no server — all new features are vanilla HTML/CSS/JS.
Surgical JSON loading principle: load one data file at a time, never bulk-load the whole 5etools corpus.
New JSONs (statstation.json, trap.json) feed the hub without duplicating data already in apps.json.

sessions.summary: Consolidated all 86 .md source files in void/assets/content/ into a single void.json (48 entries after deduplication). Added marked.js v15 + DOMPurify rendering to replace the hand-rolled toProse() function. Added comprehensive markdown prose CSS: headings with violet border-left treatment matching the hub's section-title grammar, goldaccent blockquotes, custom list markers, VOID-signature styled as a watermark, inline code with violet tint, and responsive prose sizing.
decisions: 86 .md files had mixed formats: 39 ID-named (XXXXXX.md, no front matter), 47 slug-named (toml front matter with title/date). The slug files were the original CMS export; ID files were added later.

void.json had one duplicate ID entry (9V1IW3 appeared twice — A Lot on My Mind and Panic). The Panic entry was renamed to 9ZHWIT per its VOID signature. The orphan A Lot on My Mind variant was dropped (2P13K1 is the canonical entry).
RV9S5D.md content matched LEGACY entry — skipped as duplicate.
9V1IW3.md (extra A Lot on My Mind draft) dropped since 2P13K1 is canonical.
10 genuinely new entries added from slug-only files: Big Three, Education, Focus, Hello World, Identity, Intentions, Revive, Ruthless, Therapy, Trip Report 01.
marked v15 uses extension API for custom block tokens — VOID-XXXXXX signature lines handled via custom block extension, not renderer override.
Regular script tags for CDN shards load before type=module app.js — globals are available at parse time.
rules_reinforced: All content sources (flat files, CMS exports) should be consolidated into the JSON data layer — no parallel flat-file sources
void/ is self-contained, no glass/ dependency — CDN shards loaded directly in void/index.html, not via config.json fetch
DOMPurify ALLOWED_ATTR must include 'class' for custom extension class names to survive sanitization

sessions.summary: Complete ground-up redesign of void/ — the personal blog/emotional journal page. New design is data-driven (renders all 39 entries from void.json), fully self-contained (no glass/ dependency), and built to match a precise aesthetic brief: deep-water dark, eerie unsettling texture, spotlight overlay per entry, custom cursor, scroll-reveal. The old design had hardcoded entries and mock data — now everything is live from the JSON.
decisions: No glass/style/style.css dependency — void is self-contained, its own palette, no hub design bleed
Feed sorted newest-first by date field in void.json
3-line snippet clamp with 'descend into this →' lure instead of generic Read More button
Spotlight uses curtain + panel pattern with blur backdrop, not a full-page route
Grain layer uses SVG feTurbulence fractalNoise at 0.038 opacity — subtle enough to doubt
Three slow-drifting radial shapes (70/90/55s animation) give the sense of something in the dark
VOID letters breathe individually with staggered animation-delay
Uplink node coloured in void palette (dark crimson) not hub palette (violet)
rules_reinforced: Self-contained apps define their own palette — do not inherit from glass/ variables
esc() helper must be used on all data before innerHTML injection

sessions.summary: Lunchbox pass — structural tidying with no functional changes. Loose root images moved to glass/assets/, GEMINI.md files and plandir notes consolidated into .ai/ (gitignored), dev scratch data moved out of production data layer, underdark staging data tucked into data/_drafts/, char-editor.css consolidated into the @import chain instead of a standalone <link> tag.
decisions: pfp.png and preview.png were unreferenced in code — safe to move to glass/assets/
All AI context files (.gem/plandir, GEMINI.md root + underdark) consolidated into single .ai/ hidden dot-folder
glass/data/llm-toimplement.json is dev scratch, not production data — moved to .ai/
underdark/data/_drafts/ created for characters-tobe.json and lorebooks-tobe.json (staging content, not live data)
char-editor.css was the only stylesheet loaded as a standalone <link> tag outside the @import chain — inconsistency fixed
rules_reinforced: Production data directories should contain only live, referenced data — scratch and staging files belong elsewhere
AI context / planning artifacts go in .ai/ (gitignored) not scattered across the repo root or app folders
All stylesheet loading should flow through the CSS @import chain, not rogue <link> tags

sessions.summary: Replaced every relative path traversal (../../glass/, ../glass/) with absolute root paths (/glass/...) across all 13 source files. core.js loadCoreData() base parameter removed — all fetches now hardcoded to /glass/data/. llm.js initLLM() base parameter removed. Shard fallback paths updated to /shards/<filename>. Navigation hrefs updated to / instead of ../ or ../index.html.
decisions: All cross-app paths use /absolute/root/paths — not relative traversals. Rationale: moves never break paths, every reference is self-documenting.
core.js loadCoreData() no longer accepts a base argument — callers that passed '../' updated to call loadCoreData() with no args.
llm.js initLLM() no longer accepts { base } option — the one caller (playground/script/main.js) updated to call initLLM() with no args.
Shard fallback paths changed from ${base}shards/lucide.min.js to /shards/lucide.min.js — consistent with absolute path policy.
rules_reinforced: Never use relative path traversals for cross-directory references. Use /absolute/root/paths. This rule now applies to all new code in this repo.

sessions.summary: Full repository directory restructure. playground/characters/ promoted to top-level underdark/ (shorter URL /underdark/, cleaner ../../glass/ paths vs prior ../../../../glass/). art/glass/ renamed art/assets/ and radar/glass/ renamed radar/assets/ to eliminate confusing inner 'glass' namespace on self-contained apps. Empty placeholder directories shared/ and universe/ removed. All cross-references updated: index.html files, JS imports, fetch() paths, apps.json, status/index.html, logs.json architecture section.
decisions: underdark/ lives at top level — /underdark/ URL, not /playground/characters/. This gives it the same status as /art/, /radar/, /status/.
playground/ remains for the Neural Uplink AI assistant at /playground/ and becomes the container for future app additions at /playground/<appname>/.

art/glass/ and radar/glass/ renamed to art/assets/ and radar/assets/ — these apps are fully self-contained and have no relation to the hub glass/ design system. The name 'glass' inside them was misleading.
shared/ and universe/ removed — both were empty with no content or wired-up code referencing them.
All JS import paths in underdark/script/modules/ updated from ../../../../glass/ to ../../glass/ — 4 levels to 2.
rules_reinforced: URL cleanliness: prefer top-level slugs for fully-featured apps (no nesting unless it's truly a sub-feature of the parent route).
Folder naming: self-contained sub-apps should not inherit the hub's design system folder name — use 'assets/' for clarity.

sessions.summary: Three fixes + one feature. (1) Thread images still vanishing — the placeholder-swap codepath at line 4321 called _injectImageMessageInto directly (bypassing _injectImageMessage), so addMessage was never called and the image was never written to state/IDB. Fixed by calling addMessage first, passing the msg.id through. (2) Wallhaven Gallery was only reachable via header overflow menu — added #btn-wh-profile button to Terminal Profile tab, wired to window.openWallhavenGallery(char.name, id). (3) Terminal sidebar visual redesign: tab bar gets glowing pill tabs with active bottom scan-line; profile avatar gets pulsing outer ring + gradient name; pd-block labels get auto-extending accent divider lines; profile action buttons get shimmer overlay and enhanced glow on hover. (4) Confirmed 'Export Session' in overflow is a plain-text export distinct from the JSON export in Profile — both kept.
decisions: Two export buttons are not redundant — overflow exports plain text (readable), Profile exports JSON (importable). Both kept.
Wallhaven was in overflow menu — moved/added to Profile tab so character context is already set when opened.
Terminal v2 focuses on depth through layered light — no extra DOM elements, all effect through CSS pseudo-elements and gradients.
rules_reinforced: Every fresh image inject path must go through addMessage — _injectImageMessageInto is display-only, not a persistence path.

sessions.summary: Two follow-up fixes: (1) Thread top padding was still showing large gap — root cause was CSS cache: arena.css had ?v=8 which hadn't been bumped after the padding change, so the browser served the old file. Bumped arena.css to v=9 and style.css to v=79 to force reload. (2) Profile button was a fixed bottom-left floating element — moved inside the left sidebar (sidebar__profile-row) so it naturally hides when the sidebar collapses. Flyout kept outside sidebar (sibling in DOM) as position:fixed to escape the sidebar's overflow:hidden clip; opened via JS getBoundingClientRect positioning.
decisions: Profile flyout must be a DOM sibling of the sidebar, not a child, because sidebar has overflow:hidden which clips absolutely-positioned children.
CSS version query strings must be bumped whenever styles change, or browsers will serve stale cached files.
rules_reinforced: Always bump CSS @import version strings (?v=N) when changing a stylesheet — GitHub Pages and browser caches will serve the old file otherwise.
Elements that must overflow their parent's clip boundary should be siblings in the DOM, not children.

sessions.summary: Six-bug fix session from user-reported screenshot issues. (1) arena.css: overflow:hidden on .arena__content created scroll container that clipped thread top — changed to overflow:clip. (2) syncConfigUI persona fields used raw field access that returned undefined for uninitialised config keys — fixed with ?? ''. (3) #create-character button in Roster sidebar footer existed in HTML but had no event handler — wired to char-editor:open dispatch. (4) showPickerModal generateFn had no timeout — added 30s Promise.race timeout with user-friendly error message. (5) _injectImageMessage never called addMessage so images weren't persisted to history — fresh injects now call addMessage('image', dataUrl) which auto-promotes to IDB in state.js. (6) Standalone #uplink-node floating button and its inline click script removed — Uplink Home is already in the profile flyout.
decisions: overflow:clip (not overflow:hidden) is correct for layout containers that need to contain children without creating a scroll context.
All config UI fields that might be unset must use ?? '' to avoid 'undefined' string in inputs.
#create-character was already in the HTML footer of the Roster view — just needed the event handler wired.
showPickerModal timeout set to 30s — long enough to not false-positive on slow connections but short enough to not look like a hang.
IDB promotion for injected images is handled in state.js addMessage — ui.js only needs to call addMessage and the persistence is automatic.
UPLINK floating button removed — no functionality lost since profile flyout has identical nav.
rules_reinforced: overflow:clip prevents layout bleed without creating a scroll stacking context — prefer it over overflow:hidden for non-scroll containers.
Every async generateFn passed to showPickerModal must be guarded by the 30s timeout — never assume network will respond.

sessions.summary: Fixed bot message avatar fallback in appendMessage: buildAvatarHtml was called without charMeta so _charEmoji(null) returned generic 👤 even for known characters whose IDB avatar hadn't resolved. Now passes meta || char so character-appropriate emoji derives from tags/name. Also noted: screenshot admin button visual discrepancy (Re-describe/Entrance/Irony appearing lighter) is a rendering artifact — class names in HTML confirmed correct (.codex-admin-btn on all). Third screenshot (mobile dot + user icon) appears to be native browser UI, not Underdark DOM.
decisions: Avatar fallback should always use charMeta when available — even a default 👤 is better than an empty circle but a matching emoji is better still.
Admin button visual differences in screenshot are a contrast artifact of the mobile viewport, not a CSS bug — no change needed.
rules_reinforced: Always pass charMeta to buildAvatarHtml when the character is known, even when avatar is expected to be present.

sessions.summary: Image Studio generation history: _studioHistory[] session array (max 20 entries, [{src, prompt, model, ts}]). Each successful generate pushes to the front. _renderStudioHistory() renders a horizontal thumbnail strip #studio-history-strip in a #studio-history-wrap section between the gallery strip and preview area. Click any thumbnail to restore image + prompt into the preview. Current entry highlighted. img-studio.css: .studio-history-wrap, .studio-history-strip, .studio-history-item, .studio-history-thumb. Batch F item 3 of 3 — all Batch F complete.
decisions: History is session-local (not persisted to state) to avoid unbounded growth — existing gallery[] already persists all generated images.
Clicking a history item also restores the prompt text into #studio-prompt-ta so the user can iterate from that exact configuration.
Max 20 entries — oldest are dropped from history (still in gallery[]) to keep the strip tight.
rules_reinforced: Session state (not persisted) is appropriate for ephemeral UI aids like history strips — don't conflate it with durable state.

sessions.summary: Video on-demand: videoGallery[] added to ensureGalleryStore alongside gallery[] and galleryMeta. vg-inject now also saves to videoGallery automatically; new vg-save-gallery button saves without injecting into thread. Profile tab renders a video-strip (last 4 film-icon tiles, click to play) and a 'Video Gallery' button that opens #modal-video-gallery. Modal has two views: grid (thumbnail tiles + overlay play/download/delete) and inline player (video element + back/download/delete). renderVideoStrip(id) called on profile load alongside renderGalleryStrip. Batch F item 2 of 3.
decisions: Auto-save on inject (not just explicit 'save to gallery') — if you add a video to the thread you clearly want it persisted.
Video thumbnails are film-icon tiles, not <video> poster frames — avoids network requests for thumbnails that may be remote URLs.
Player view is inline within the modal (grid hidden, player shown) rather than a separate lightbox — simpler for a single-file video viewer.
rules_reinforced: Any media that a user generates should be auto-persisted to the appropriate gallery store — don't make the user remember to save.

sessions.summary: Taggable gallery: ensureGalleryStore now initialises galleryMeta ({} map from ref → {tags[]}) alongside gallery []. renderGalleryModal rebuilt to render tag chips on each item (hover-visible strip), inline tag-add input (Enter to commit, blur to commit, Escape to cancel), tag-remove click, and a filter bar above the grid. tagFilter param propagates through re-renders so filter state is preserved after add/remove. gallery-modal.css gains .gallery-tag-filter, .gallery-tag-chip + modifier variants (--filter, --item, --add, --input). Batch F item 1 of 3.
decisions: galleryMeta stored as a ref-keyed object (not array) so lookups are O(1) and deletions clean up naturally with 'delete obj[ref]'.
Tag chips visible on hover only (same pattern as overlay buttons) to keep the grid clean at default zoom.
Filter bar only renders when at least one tag exists across the gallery — hidden otherwise to avoid empty UI chrome.
tagFilter param passed through all re-render calls so add/remove operations inside a filtered view don't reset to All.
rules_reinforced: Parallel metadata stores (galleryMeta alongside gallery) are the right pattern for enriching flat arrays without breaking existing consumers.
Always propagate view state (tagFilter) through re-render calls so the UI doesn't lose context on mutation.

sessions.summary: Thread lorebook pins: pinnedLoreBookIds surfaced in Thread Config modal as a lorebook checklist; llm-engine.js buildPayload now marks pinned book entries alwaysOn before scanLorebooks so they always inject regardless of keyword matching. Relationship map: canvas overlay in Scene Codex footer (git-branch button), circular node layout, draws avatar/initial nodes and dashed edges with wrapped label text, reads from groupConfig.relationships. Both are Batch E items from the feature backlog.
decisions: Relationship map uses circular layout (not force-directed) — simpler, no physics loop, looks equally good for 2-8 nodes.
Lore pins modify a copy of the lore array per-call (not mutating state) so pinning is thread-local and stateless in the engine.
Canvas draws synchronously on requestAnimationFrame so avatars may show initials on first open if IDB async hasn't resolved — acceptable.
rules_reinforced: Dead schema fields in state.js must have both UI to set them and engine code to consume them — otherwise they silently do nothing.

sessions.summary: Auto-save system: _autoSaveInstance() fires every N turns (default 20), exports full instance to localStorage underdark_autosave, shows subtle toast. Settings panel gets an interval input (0=off) and a download button. Ctrl+K command palette: overlay with fuzzy-search across 24 defined commands, arrow/enter keyboard navigation, backdrop close, Escape dismiss. Both features implement Batch G infrastructure items. Prior session also completed Phase 6 item 10 (auto entrance on character join).
decisions: Auto-save writes to localStorage (not a download) to avoid intrusive file-save dialogs mid-session; the download button in settings is the manual retrieval path.
Command palette uses original-index tracking (data-cp-orig) so filtered results still dispatch the correct action regardless of query.
24 commands in palette include all single-key shortcuts plus common modal-openers and codex actions — no new features, just discovery surface.
rules_reinforced: Filter-then-dispatch: always track original array index when rendering filtered lists, not filtered position.
Ctrl+K must be intercepted before the inInput guard since it applies even when a textarea has focus.

sessions.summary: Audited Phase 6 plan against live code and found all 20 items were already implemented except item 10 (auto entrance on character join). Implemented that final item: addCharacterToThread now auto-fires _fireOverlord('entrance') when a character joins a session with existing history, controlled by a new autoEntrance narrative flag (default on). FLAG_KEYS updated; HTML checkbox added to Narrative Flags grid.
decisions: All 20 Phase 6 features confirmed done — no ghost items.
Auto entrance fires only when history.length > 0 so it never interferes with fresh thread first_mes flow.
autoEntrance defaults to true (opt-out model) since it's the desired-on case — checked in HTML, flag.?? true in syncConfigUI.
rules_reinforced: Read live code before assuming prior sessions completed all stated intent — plan items can be stale.
Flags use ?? true default in syncConfigUI; new flags that default on need checked attribute in HTML.

The card was placed into playground/characters/data/cards/ and added alphabetically to the main index.json roster. Removed the character from the to-be queue.
decisions: Implemented full Underdark extensions for GLaDOS according to chara_card_v2 spec.
Sorted characters alphabetically during insertion into the main roster index.
Set avatar_path to null to allow UI fallback rendering.
rules_reinforced: Zero-build steps philosophy observed. Characters are pure JSON manifests.
Sovereign node aesthetic: characters must have deep traits, triggers, and psychological tags consistent with a mature setting.

sessions.summary: Full redesign of the Scene Codex (god-view overlay) with character starring. Replaced the 3-column flat layout with a horizontal cast strip + 4-column body + footer stats strip. Added star/favorite per character stored in state.config._starredBots. Meters redesigned with directional color names and subtle tick marks. Scene Ledger changed from a grid to stacked cards. Session stats moved to a clean footer row. Cast strip is DM/group mode aware.
decisions: Cast strip renders at the top of the codex above the columns — character avatars with star toggle buttons and active-speaker ring
Star state persists in state.config._starredBots as a string array — same persistence model as other config keys
renderCodexCast() is a hoisted function declaration inside initUI() — called from openCodex(), updateCodexDigest(), and the end of renderActiveBots()
Digest column given its own dedicated column (col 3) — no longer buried inside God Controls column
Footer close button replaces old bottom-center tab — lives in codex-footer strip alongside session stats
DM mode adds scene-codex--dm class, group mode adds scene-codex--group — CSS targets these for minor layout differences
rules_reinforced: All visual state toggles use data attributes or class toggling — no inline style hacks
Function declarations (hoisted) used for functions called before their definition line
