sessions.summary: Session continuation from Batch C context overflow. Completed all 30 character system_prompt_suffix updates (fixed pre-existing JSON syntax error in rebecca.json — muscleTone value had a parenthetical annotation outside quotes). Implemented profile flyout (NanoGPT-inspired avatar button next to Uplink, opens a flyout with Settings/My Persona/Backup/Uplink Home links). Settings modal restructured: Interface tab removed (Arena Background belongs to Chat Tint overlay), Generation panel massively expanded (Typical P, TFS-Z, Epsilon/ETA cutoffs, Seed with roll+lock), Neural panel gains Wallhaven API key section with get-key link. Thread Settings moved from persistent header button into the More overflow dropdown. Profile flyout also updates display name from active continuity config.userName.
decisions: Profile flyout position: bottom-left at 3.6rem from left, next to Uplink — mirrors the NanoGPT sidebar bottom-left avatar pattern but adapted to fixed overlay layout.
Interface tab removed from Settings entirely — Arena Background is already accessible via Chat Tint overlay (More menu) which is the right place for it per-thread.

Wallhaven API key in Settings Neural panel syncs to localStorage.getItem('wh_apikey') — same key used by wallhaven.js module; saves directly to localStorage so wallhaven picks it up on next open without requiring a page reload.
Generation parameters grouped into 3 sections: Sampling (core params), Penalties (rep/presence/freq/epsilon/eta), Context & Output — easier to scan than a flat grid.
Seed control: text input + dice roll button + lock button — lock toasts the locked value, empty clears seed to null (random per call).
Thread Settings hidden from arena header (moved to overflow) to reduce header clutter — Settings button also removed from header since profile flyout replaces it.
rules_reinforced: JSON values cannot contain inline parenthetical annotations — 8 (Cybernetic) is invalid; must be a string: '8 (Cybernetic)'.
When removing DOM elements that have JS listeners, use optional chaining on all querySelector calls — removal is safe if the JS uses ?. throughout.

sessions.summary: Batch C executed (continued from Phase 7 session). Living World stream mode: characters post autonomously on a configurable timer (15s/30s/60s/2m), each post AI-generated via fetchCompletion with in-character prompt, injected at top of feed with typewriter animation + cursor blink. Start/Pause controlled from social sidebar (Living World button) and in-feed bar. Post-from-thread: bot message action button (image-plus icon) AI-generates a caption from the message snippet and saves a local feed post. Fixed a ctx object literal syntax bug (ctx.galleryCharId: null → galleryCharId: null) found on review.
decisions: Living World uses fetchCompletion (non-streaming) for post generation — streaming into a typewriter makes the typewriter feel redundant; a completed post typed out character-by-character is the right UX.
Speed control lives in the sidebar select (not a slider) — 4 named presets (Fast/Normal/Slow/Chill) with ±20% jitter are cleaner than a continuous range for a social feed cadence.
Post-from-thread uses claude-haiku model for caption generation — fast + cheap, caption is only 1-2 sentences.
Stream bar in feed arena (purple pulsing indicator) communicates live mode is active without interrupting the feed scroll area.
rules_reinforced: Object literal property names must be plain identifiers — ctx.galleryCharId as a property key is invalid JS syntax (parser sees it as a computed property name that fails).
Living World stop must clearTimeout AND null the timer ref to prevent double-fire if resumed quickly.

sessions.summary: Phase 7 begins. User answered 20 design questions defining 7 batches of work. Batch A executed: ctx object introduced at top of initUI() centralising all cross-section mutable state — galleryCharId, feedMode/permanentFeedPosts, activeSidebarTab, studio state, oracle state, $chatArena/$feedArena DOM refs. This is the architectural prerequisite for future module splits (social-feed.js, image-studio.js, thread-engine.js, codex.js). Batch B executed: right sidebar tabs renamed Character/World/Settings → Profile/Codex/Config with lucide icons; responsive.css was already written anticipating this rename. Tab buttons now support inline icons via flex display.
decisions: ctx object pattern chosen over immediate physical file split — coupling is too deep to safely split without the ctx layer first; this pass is zero-behaviour-change refactor only.
Right sidebar tabs renamed semantically: Profile (character card), Codex (world+lorebooks), Config (settings+persona) — names match content purpose, not implementation detail.
Tab buttons given icons via data-lucide attributes — tab identity is now visual as well as textual, especially important on mobile where label space is tight.

Phase 7 batches defined: A (ctx refactor), B (sidebar), C (living social feed), D (immersion upgrades), E (world+characters), F (image+gallery), G (infrastructure) — each subsequent session executes one batch.
rules_reinforced: Never split modules before establishing a shared context object — closure coupling will break call sites silently.
Semantic naming of UI sections reduces cognitive friction — 'Codex' is a better name than 'World' for a section containing lorebooks and world lore.

sessions.summary: Hard reset button added to continuity config. Subtle ghost icon button (.re-reset-btn) in reality editor footer — muted by default, red-tint hover hint. Clicking opens a custom three-option inline dialog (Keep Images / Wipe Everything / Cancel) without confirm(). Wipe path: clearApiKey(), localStorage.clear(), sessionStorage.clear(), then idbClear(). Keep Images path: idbGetAllEntries() filtered to img: prefix keys, idbClear(), idbSetBulk(imageEntries). All CSS added to controls.css (.re-reset-btn, .hard-reset-prompt*, animation hrpIn). controls.css bumped to ?v=2, style.css?v=71. Also fixed API key singleton bug from previous session — image-engine.js and commands.js imported llm-auth.js bare (no ?v=), browser split them from ui.js's ?v=3 instance causing setApiKey/getApiKey to operate on separate module instances; unified all imports to ?v=3 across all 7 modules.
decisions: Three-option dialog (not confirm()) required because two binary prompts (really reset? then keep images?) felt clunky — a single custom dialog with three named actions is cleaner UX.
Button is subtle by design — opacity 0.35 at rest, red only on hover — hard reset is accessible but not accidentally triggerable.
Image preservation filters on img: key prefix only (not avatar: prefix) — generated images are user content worth keeping; avatars are typically small and easily re-generated.
Inline dialog via DOM append (not a modal system) — avoids requiring modal infrastructure to be ready for a destructive action that fires during configuration.
rules_reinforced: All importers of a shared ES module must use identical URL strings — mismatched ?v= strings cause the browser to create separate module instances and break singleton state.
Destructive actions must have a cancel escape, not just a confirmation — three options beats two binary modals.

sessions.summary: Quality pass with three deliverables. Thread Config gear: #btn-thread-config added to arena header (settings-2 icon), opens #modal-thread-config with Core tab (thread scenario, model override, temp/maxOutput inherit toggles, player name/persona overrides, auto-attach lorebooks) and Tone tab (full narrativeTone fields with quick pills matching thread setup wizard). Gear shows accent dot-badge when any overrides are active. Memory compression summaries: summarizeDroppedMessages() added to llm-engine.js — background LLM call on dropped messages produces 3-5 bullet memory digest, cached in sessionStorage per chatId+horizonMsgId, injected below context-horizon divider as styled .context-horizon__summary block. codex-meters.js module: all meter logic (keyword scoring, LLM scoring, DOM sync, status tag parsing) extracted from ui.js closure into a proper ES module using init() dependency injection; ui.js retains thin shims for backward-compatible call sites. Module split audit confirmed: social feed, image studio, and other UI sections cannot be extracted without a context-object refactor — deferred as a future architectural phase.
decisions: Thread config gear surfaces in header (not just context menu) — in-chat access is the primary use case; users shouldn't need to leave the thread to adjust settings.
Memory summaries are background-async and patch in when ready — thread render is never blocked by the LLM call.

codex-meters.js uses init() dependency injection (not re-importing state/qs directly) — preserves the zero-build philosophy while enabling testability; modules receive live references so state mutations are reflected.
Module split of UI sections (social feed, image studio) deferred — closure coupling is too deep for safe extraction without a context-object pattern refactor; not worth the risk in a quality pass.
rules_reinforced: Module extraction requires pure logic/data separation — UI sections that share closure state cannot be split without a context-object refactor.
Background async operations must never block synchronous render paths.

sessions.summary: Batch B — 11 features across immersion, scene management, and information display. Player bubble UFR rendering: rp-speech/action/thought/narration classes applied at 78% intensity for user messages (lighter variant) so bot responses stay visually dominant; players get monogram initial avatars instead of generic emoji. Scene markers: sceneBreak field in message schema, auto-set for all Overlord messages; scene-divider element (purple gradient pill) injected before each Overlord block in both live and history-replay modes, numbered sequentially. Message variant navigator: retry preserves old content in variants[], chevron prev/next navigator with turn counter renders below bot messages when variants exist. Structured codex digest: replaced plain text excerpt with live character rows (22px avatar, name in mono, last spoken line truncated to 80 chars), Overlord mode badge, scene number badge; session stats strip gains Scene counter. Auto transition: _maybeAutoTransition fires after each bot turn — if turns%N==0 and no Overlord block in last N messages, auto-fires transition narration. Memory horizon UI: context cut point computed from token budget, rendered as amber lock-icon divider; out-of-context messages faded to 35% opacity with sepia filter, hoverable to 60%. Memory depth bumped to 20. Scene ledger: 4 structured slots replace single arc input — Arc, Secret, Reveal, Relationship; stored in state.config._codexLedger; injected into buildOverlordContext (Overlord-only, not every bot message per user decision). Character-targeted directives: codex inject + reinject tray buttons show showPickerModal char list in group mode; All characters option maps to 'the character' string. AI contextual quick replies: background fetchCompletion after each bot turn generates 4 reply starters, cached in _aiQRCache; displayed as warm-gold italic chips above static QR groups when bar opens. Structured prompt inspector: showSystemPrompt flag now renders each payload message as a collapsible <details> block with role badge (colour-coded), section label (from [HEADER] or message number), and token count; total token budget shown in summary. Scene number tracking: _countScenes() counts overlord-mode history entries, displayed in cstat-scene stat strip, scene divider labels, and passed as sceneNumber into buildOverlordContext.
decisions: Player UFR at lighter variant (78% intensity) rather than full — bot responses must stay the visual focal point; symmetric UFR would dilute the character's presence.
Scene ledger injection is Overlord-only (not every bot message) — individual character responses stay lean; the Overlord is the narrative meta-layer and should hold meta-state.
AI quick replies auto-generate after each bot turn (not on-demand) — the cache means zero wait time when the bar opens; background calls are cheap at 120 tokens.
Variant navigation uses a flat [variants..., currentContent] array — navigating rearranges the content and variants fields so the 'current' content is always msgObj.content, not a separate pointer.
_countScenes() reads from state.history overlordMode field rather than DOM .overlord-block count — DOM is not available in llm-engine.js context; history is the ground truth.
Auto transition default N=8 stored in state.config.autoTransitionEvery — configurable without a UI element yet; default 8 feels like 2-3 exchanges per character.
Memory horizon uses a local token budget estimate (text.length/4) — exact same formula as applyContextStrategy, no need to import estimateTokens into ui.js.
Character picker for targeted directives shows All characters as first option — most use case is directing a specific character, but scene-wide directives should remain easy.
rules_reinforced: Lighter intensity for player UFR vs bot UFR is correct — characters are the stars, the player is the author.
Scene ledger meta-state belongs to the Overlord system prompt, not character prompts.
Background async calls (AI quick replies, LLM meter scoring) must never block or show errors to the user — silent catch everywhere.
DOM is always the source of truth for live values; state.history is the source of truth for persistent structural values.

sessions.summary: Foundation batch — five LLM information architecture improvements. (1) rp-action visual layer: *asterisk* text now renders with blue-silver glow as a distinct 4th format tier (Physical action) alongside speech/thought/narration; UFR directive explicitly enumerates all four layers with examples. (2) STATUS tag meter feedback: bot responses can now contain [TENSION X%]/[INTIMACY X%]/[DANGER X%] tags that directly update the codex meters live during a session. (3) LLM-scored meters: hybrid scoring system — keyword heuristic fires immediately, background LLM call every 3 turns returns structured {tension,intimacy,danger} JSON and overwrites the estimate; result cached in sessionStorage per history length. (4) Enriched group cue card: the per-character turn anchor now includes the last spoken line from every other character and a live meter state string so the LLM has full situational context at write time. (5) Narrative tone in DMs: tone config section moved from Group-only tab to Generation tab (always visible); llm-engine.js injects a [NARRATIVE TONE DIRECTIVE] system message for DM threads when fields are set; tone collected once in thread setup and mirrored into gc for group threads to avoid double DOM reads.
decisions: <em>→rp-action conversion runs post-sanitize, after the text-node narration pass, so there is no interference between the two passes — narration regex was updated from excluding <em> to excluding .rp-action spans.
Meter DOM is the source of truth for meters in buildPayload — llm-engine.js has no DOM access, so triggerBotResponse reads #codex-tension-val etc. and passes them as ctx.meters.
Narrative tone section belongs in the Generation tab (not Group tab) because it is fundamentally a prompt configuration, not a group-specific setting — DM threads have tone as much as group chats do.
Tone fields collected once into tc.narrativeTone before the group check in thread setup; group threads mirror from tc to avoid double DOM reads and prevent potential mismatch.
LLM meter scoring uses sessionStorage keyed by history length to avoid scoring the same history twice — this keeps background calls cheap.
STATUS tags are parsed after marked() rendering but before updateCodexDigest() so the meters update synchronously with the message appearing.
rules_reinforced: All LLM format layers must be in UFR — if a new visual tier is added (rp-action), the UFR directive must be updated to name it explicitly with examples.
Post-sanitize DOM manipulation is the correct place for format-layer conversions when marked.js transforms raw markup before our rendering pass runs.
Meter DOM is the single source of truth for live meter values — never read from state or a cache when the DOM is available.
Narrative tone is a thread-level prompt concern, not a group-only concern.

sessions.summary: LLM information architecture overhaul. Three pillars: (1) Structured player identity — the player now has a proper character sheet (appearance, mood, role, status) injected into every character's system prompt via buildPlayerBlock, replacing the single free-text 'About User' paragraph; characters can react to who the player physically and emotionally is. (2) Overlord context awareness — _fireOverlord now passes scenario tone, character roster, player name/role/appearance, and live meter values (tension/intimacy/danger) into a richly structured system prompt; the Overlord LLM writes narration calibrated to the actual scene state rather than generic descriptions. (3) Three new Overlord modes added — 'entrance' (character arrival narration, emerald), 'whisper' (private aside only the player perceives, silver), 'irony' (dramatic irony injection, amber-orange) — each with codex button, unique prompt, and CSS palette. UFR moved to the top of all system prompts so format rules have maximum weight. responseFormat in buildAIDirectivesBlock suppressed to prevent markup conflicts.
decisions: buildPlayerBlock lives in llm-engine.js alongside all other system prompt builders — it receives config (not state) so it's testable and decoupled from the DOM.
Player fields (playerAppearance, playerMood, playerRole, playerStatus) stored in state.config alongside userName/userPersona — same persistence layer, same syncConfigUI pattern.
buildOverlordContext exported from llm-engine.js as a pure function — ui.js calls it with live DOM state (meter values) to produce the context string; keeps llm-engine.js dependency-free.
Meter values read from DOM (#codex-tension-val etc.) rather than state — the meters are updated live by updateCodexMeters and the DOM is the most current source.

UFR placed at the very start of the system prompt (before character identity). Rationale: models weight earlier content more heavily; format rules must survive the entire subsequent character content.

responseFormat in buildAIDirectivesBlock suppressed with a comment explaining WHY (asterisk/italic modes would conflict with UFR's double-quote/tilde/asterisk syntax) — the field is preserved for prose style hints but not emitted.
Whisper mode uses italic font-style and reduced opacity in CSS to signal its private nature visually — it looks different from other Overlord blocks at a glance.
Entrance mode uses the activeBotId (single character) or full botIds list (group) — the button is universal and contextual.
rules_reinforced: Player identity is a character sheet, not a text blob — the LLM reacts better to structured fields than unformatted prose.
Overlord narration must be scene-aware — injecting scenario, characters, and meters into its system prompt is mandatory for coherent output.
UFR must lead the system prompt — placing it after character identity allows creative content to dilute the format directive.
Never conflict markup specs — if UFR defines format, suppress any other format injection that could override it.

sessions.summary: Phase 5 — Underdark control overhaul and format system. Four major pillars implemented: (1) Force-trigger: zap pill buttons in group chats + overflow 'Force Response' in DM allow the user to manually fire any character without sending a user message; (2) Character isolation: three-layer system prevents the LLM from writing as the wrong character — GROUP ISOLATION system prompt block, full history attribution (all messages prefixed Speaker: Content in group mode), and a post-history anchor message; (3) UFR (Underdark Format Ruleset): a format directive injected into every character's system prompt specifying exactly how to wrap speech/action/thought/narration; tilde thought rendering added to renderMarkdown alongside legacy underscore; (4) Overlord expansion: _fireOverlord centralises all narrator LLM calls, four new modes (transition/reaction/moment/recap) with distinct CSS palettes injected via _codexAdminBtn boilerplate eliminator, overlordMode persisted to message schema and restored on full history re-render.
decisions: Force-trigger bypasses the user message send path entirely — calls clearGroupTimers() then triggerBotResponse() directly so no phantom user message appears in history.

Character isolation uses three layers rather than one because any single layer can be ignored by the LLM under creative pressure — system prompt block + history attribution + anchor message together create a strong enough signal.
UFR uses tilde (~) for thoughts because underscore is already ambiguous in markdown; the renderMarkdown function now handles both patterns to cover legacy history and new UFR-formatted responses.
_fireOverlord accepts a promptFn callback that receives {charNames, scenario, histText} — caller defines the narrative instruction, helper handles the LLM call and DOM/state injection.
_codexAdminBtn abstracts the disable/spinner/re-enable pattern that was repeated verbatim for every Overlord button — five buttons now wired in five lines.
overlordMode stored in message.meta so renderFullHistory can reconstruct the correct visual mode when re-rendering from history rather than defaulting all Overlord blocks to 'scene'.
renderFullHistory cleanup selector changed from '.message' to '.message, .overlord-block' to prevent stale live-injected blocks from persisting alongside history-reconstructed ones.
rules_reinforced: The LLM cannot be trusted to stay in character in group mode without explicit isolation — GROUP ISOLATION block + turn anchor are mandatory for all group payloads.
All Overlord LLM calls must go through _fireOverlord — no direct fetchCompletion calls for narrator content.
overlordMode must be saved to message history at injection time, not inferred at render time.
Force-trigger must check state.isStreaming and getApiKey() before firing — same guards as the normal send path.

sessions.summary: Phase 3: full instance export/import and LLM response formatting cleanup. storage.js gained idbGetAllEntries (IDB cursor walk returning all key/value pairs) and idbSetBulk (bulk transaction write). state.js gained exportFullInstance (serialises all state + IDB blobs to underdark_full_v1 JSON) and importFullInstance (full restore: bulk IDB write, state overwrite, sanitize, save). UI: 'Full Backup' section in Settings panel with Export All and Import All buttons; import guarded by confirm dialog; Import All has subtle red tint. shared-utils.js gained parseLLMArray, parseLLMJson, parseLLMLines — the three patterns that were copy-pasted across 7 call sites in ui.js, now centralised. Also noted export/import infrastructure now satisfies the prerequisite for the auto-save system (deferred but unblocked).
decisions: IDB bulk export uses a cursor walk rather than individual gets — single transaction, no per-key round trips, handles arbitrarily large blob stores.
Import uses idbSetBulk which opens a single readwrite transaction for all puts — more reliable than sequential idbSet calls which would each open their own transaction.
parseLLMArray/parseLLMJson/parseLLMLines go in shared-utils.js, not llm-engine.js — they operate on response strings after the fact, not on the request/response protocol itself.
parseLLMLines strips leading bullets, numbers, dashes, and quote characters — these are the common contaminants when the model ignores 'one per line, no preamble' instructions.
The 'Import All' button is tinted red (not full danger styling) to signal that it's destructive without being alarming — the confirm() dialog is the real safety gate.
rules_reinforced: IDB bulk operations should use a single transaction per batch — opening N transactions for N items is fragile and slow.
LLM response parsers belong in shared-utils.js. Any new feature that needs to parse a structured LLM response should use parseLLMArray/parseLLMJson/parseLLMLines rather than writing inline.

sessions.summary: Post-Phase-2 audit and hotfix pass. Found and fixed: (1) 5 Phase 2 fetchCompletion calls sent maxTokens (camelCase) instead of max_tokens — OpenAI-compatible API silently ignores unknown fields, so these calls ran completely uncapped; (2) 5 model fields were state.config.model without fallback — undefined before user selects a model; (3) missing $thread.scrollTop after placeholder→real image swap. Updated all pending_work entries to 'done' with implementation notes. ui.js bumped to v=85.
decisions: fetchCompletion passes the payload object directly to JSON.stringify for the wire — the correct field name is max_tokens (OpenAI API format), NOT maxTokens (JS convention). Any new call must use max_tokens.
state.config.model can be undefined on first load before user configures anything — always add || 'deepseek-r1' fallback for non-streaming fetchCompletion calls.
All pending_work entries from Phase 2 are now marked done — confirmed by reading the actual live code, not assuming the previous session's summary was complete.
rules_reinforced: fetchCompletion payloads must use OpenAI API field names (snake_case: max_tokens, not camelCase: maxTokens) — the function passes the object directly to the wire without transformation.
Always verify pending_work statuses by reading live code after a session, not trusting the implementation summary — summaries can describe intent, not always what landed.

sessions.summary: Large multi-feature Phase 2 implementation for playground/characters (Underdark). Implemented: ♾️ prefix enforcement and permanent records for continuities and threads (rename replaces delete); group chat emoji avatar picker in creation modal with popup grid; thematic emoji fallback per character species/tags for avatars without URLs; animated image-loading placeholder injected before gen completes then swapped in-place; redo button on generated images opens 5-question style/vibe picker to guide the next generation; 3-step contextual AI scenario recommendation wizard backed by compact scenario-index.json; expanded codex god panel with 4 new inject buttons and Admin section; Overlord auto-fire on group chat open when history exists but no block present; pending_work tracking section added to logs.json with 14 entries; CSS for all new elements added to char-editor.css. Session continued from a prior compacted context via summary.
decisions: Continuities (realities) and threads (chats) are permanent — delete removed and replaced with rename to prevent accidental data loss; matches the 'permanent record' philosophy.
Compact scenario-index.json (~80 chars per entry) used for AI recommendations instead of full scenarios.json (~800-2000 chars per entry) — ~95% context reduction for recommendation calls.
3-step wizard generates each question using the LLM with previous answers as context, then requests a JSON array of 3 IDs from scenario-index.json — keeps format machine-parseable.
_charEmoji() derives emoji from character tags/species/name via regex lookup table — avoids hardcoding, handles novel species gracefully with 🧙 fallback.
Image placeholder uses CSS animation (translateX slide) instead of a real progress percentage — no progress API available, shimmer communicates 'busy' without false precision.
Generation token pattern (_groupGeneration) already in place from Phase 1 — reused and confirmed effective for isolating async group chat loops.
Overlord auto-fire checks for existing .overlord-block before firing — idempotent, won't double-inject on re-renders.
pending_work section in logs.json uses status field (not_started/in_progress/done/deferred) so future LLM instances can triage work without reading full session history.
rules_reinforced: Permanent record design: destructive actions on user-created content (chats, realities) should be replaced with safer alternatives (rename) — data loss from misclick is unrecoverable in IndexedDB.
LLM context for recommendation/classification calls should use the most compact representation that still provides enough signal — scenario summaries + tags is sufficient for matching.
Async placeholder-to-real swaps require a pre-created DOM element with a stable reference — the inner function pattern (_injectImageMessageInto) enables both fresh creation and in-place replacement.

sessions.summary: Full codebase audit of playground/characters — identified 3 critical, 5 major, 4 minor bugs and resolved all 12. Critical fixes: IDB/localStorage race in addMessage (saveState now deferred until after blob write), detached group loop replaced boolean abort flag with generation token, streaming closure now snapshots chat/reality IDs and discards responses written to wrong context. Major fixes: lucideRefresh scoped to passed node, picker modal listener leak closed, orphan placeholder removed on stream failure instead of showing un-persisted error bubble, renderMarkdownSafe > encoding restored for arrow notation in character cards. Minor: avatar cache namespace, setSendState undefined guard, reinject ordering in group queue.
decisions: IDB blob write must complete before saveState — using .finally() to guarantee exactly one save regardless of success or failure.
Boolean abort flags are unsafe for detached async IIFEs — incrementing generation tokens allow old loops to self-identify and exit without any shared mutable boolean.
Stream context must be snapshotted at call time — closures over state.chat are stale if the user switches chat while streaming.
lucideRefresh should always scope to the passed node — global createIcons() on every call scales O(n) with DOM size and is called ~98 times per active session.
On stream abort or error, the un-persisted placeholder DOM node should be removed rather than left as a visible ghost — errors surface via toast instead.
rules_reinforced: Never call lucide.createIcons() without { nodes: [el] } scoping unless at boot — unscoped calls are O(full DOM) and accumulate during streaming.
Async operations that depend on a context (chat session, reality) must snapshot the relevant IDs before going async and validate them in callbacks.
IDB writes and their corresponding state saves are a pair — never save state before the IDB write completes or the persisted record will contain stale data.

sessions.summary: Added reusable showPickerModal() utility — generates 3 AI options in a styled modal with free-text Other fallback. Wired all spark/suggest/amplify/avoid buttons in the thread setup wizard to use it instead of directly filling fields. Fixed image download (data URL → blob URL conversion prevents browser file-picker dialog). Improved scene-intro message styling with gradient background, subtle shimmer, and Narrator attribution tag. Also fixed await-in-non-async bug in streamCompletion completion callback (last-speaker background update).
decisions: showPickerModal takes a generateFn async callback that returns string[] — keeps the picker reusable for any AI-generation context without coupling it to specific LLM calls.
Picker opens immediately and shows loading state while the AI call runs — user sees activity rather than a frozen button.
Image download converts data URL to blob via fetch() then URL.createObjectURL() — avoids browser treating it as a file save location rather than an inline download.
scene-intro now has a right-aligned '— Narrator' credit and gradient background to differentiate it visually from bot messages.
rules_reinforced: Always check async/await usage in callbacks passed to non-async-aware functions — await in a plain arrow function callback is a SyntaxError.
Image downloads should always use blob URL (URL.createObjectURL) not raw data URLs — raw data URL behavior varies by browser and OS.

sessions.summary: Diagnosed and fixed blank arena / JS not loading. Root cause was a missing comma in data/index.json after Lady Jessica's avatar_path was updated last session — invalid JSON caused loadManifest() to silently fail, preventing all manifest characters from loading. Also confirmed CDN shard paths (cdn.trap.lol) are correct for production and verified all 10 JS modules return HTTP 200 on live. Clarified that local dev requires ../../shards/ paths (gitignored) per CLAUDE.md protocol while production uses cdn.trap.lol.
decisions: JSON validation must be run on any data file edited as part of a session — python3 -m json.tool is the quick check.
loadManifest() silently catches parse errors so invalid JSON in index.json doesn't crash the app but does silently drop all manifest characters.
cdn.trap.lol returns 403 for localhost origins — local dev MUST use ../../shards/ per CLAUDE.md; production uses cdn.trap.lol URLs in the committed HTML.
Committed production HTML should always use cdn.trap.lol shard paths; the CLAUDE.md protocol for local dev (swap to /shards/ temporarily, restore before push) applies here too.
rules_reinforced: Always run json.tool validation after editing any *.json file — a missing comma silently breaks manifest loading.
CLAUDE.md local dev protocol applies to index.html shard tags too: local = ../../shards/, production = cdn.trap.lol — swap before pushing, restore after.

sessions.summary: Phase 2+3 of the group chat overhaul. Added narrative tone config section to group wizard (sexual energy, tone tag multi-toggles, amplify/avoid fields, pacing — all injected as hard constraints into every group LLM response). Per-field AI spark buttons on context questions and relationship pairs. Spark ideas (4 clickable concept cards) and Generate (full narrative scene) on opening scene field. Fixed group chat UX bugs: pill clicks no longer switch the profile panel; gallery save shows a char picker in groups; last-speaker portrait updates the cinematic background after each bot turn; group right panel now shows multi-member overview with narrative tone summary. Fixed thread model override: thread-level tc.model now wins over per-character modelOverride so setting a thread to deepseek-v3.2 actually uses v3.2 instead of being overridden by Jinx's stored r1 preference.
decisions: Thread-level model (tc.model) passed as threadModelOverride to buildPayload and prioritised over override.modelOverride — thread setting is the user's explicit intent and must win.
Narrative tone fields injected as [NARRATIVE TONE DIRECTIVE] system message in every group response via llm-engine.js — treated as hard constraints, not suggestions.
Quick-fill pills use two patterns: data-target (single-select, re-click clears) and ts-quick-pill--toggle + data-field (multi-select, comma-joined into hidden input).
_tsGatherSceneContext() reads DOM labels (not key names) for isekai answers so it works for any question set including LLM-generated ones.
Last-speaker background only updates when no custom chatBackground preset/url is set — respects user's custom background choice.
Group profile panel replaces the right panel but individual chars still accessible via external-link button per member row.
rules_reinforced: Per-character modelOverride should be a fallback, not a trump card — thread-level settings always take precedence over per-char defaults.
HTML brace counting via simple string replacement is unreliable for JS files with template literals — visual code review is more reliable than automated counting.

sessions.summary: Group chat overhaul across 6 feature areas. Added: random turn mode with @mention override (1-2 char random replies); portrait blur slider in tint popup (per-chat, saves to chat config); eye-toggle on generated images (dims to near-invisible, reversible); Overlord 'Generate with AI' button in group wizard that drafts opening scene via LLM; LLM-generated character-aware world questions replacing static bank; group context in image prompt builder (all participants + relationships). Also suppressed per-character first_mes firing on group creation — opening scene flow replaces it. Added: random turn mode with @mention override (1-2 char random replies); portrait blur slider in tint popup (per-chat, saves to chat config); eye-toggle on generated images (dims to near-invisible, reversible); Overlord 'Generate with AI' button in group wizard that drafts opening scene via LLM; LLM-generated character-aware world questions replacing static bank; group context in image prompt builder (all participants + relationships). Also suppressed per-character first_mes firing on group creation — opening scene flow replaces it.
decisions: Random mode picks 1 bot with 40% chance of a second; @mentions always guarantee inclusion + optionally pull in one extra random bot.
Isekai questions are generated fresh per unique character set — cached by character ID signature so tab re-entry doesn't refetch.
LLM world question fallback to static bank on API failure so wizard never shows empty questions.
Image prompt builder detects group chat (state.activeBotIds.length > 1 && chat.type === 'group') and passes _buildGroupContext instead of single-char context.
Portrait blur slider added to tint popup as 0–80px range; updateCinematicBackground now reads portraitBlur from chat config instead of hardcoded 45px.
Per-character first_mes suppressed in groups entirely — too many characters firing simultaneously breaks group immersion.
rules_reinforced: VSCode auto-curls straight " in string/regex literals — always use RegExp constructor with \uXXXX for quote patterns.
All 14 commits from previous speech-colour debug session were local-only — always verify push status when testing on production (GitHub Pages).

sessions.summary: Chat rendering quality overhaul. Message bubbles completely restyled — bot bubbles now deep dark glass with a left accent bar that reveals on hover; user bubbles warm amber gradient; both sides have entry slide-in animations. RP text layers given a full sensory palette: speech (warm gold glow), thought (cool violet breathe animation), action/em (mid-lavender italic), narration paragraphs (dim lavender-grey via rp-narration class). System bracket tags like [SANITY 43%] tokenised to monospace accent badges. Streaming messages pulse the left accent bar during content arrival.
decisions:
rp-narration class applied post-parse via regex on

elements that contain no rp-speech, rp-thought, or — this correctly identifies pure scene-setting paragraphs without changing the markdown pipeline.
[STATUS value] bracket token detection runs BEFORE marked.js parse to survive the HTML transformation — pattern: /\[([A-Z][A-Z0-9 _\-]{0,24}(?:\s+[\d\w%\/\.\-]{1,12})?)\]/g.
Bot bubble uses ::before pseudo-element for the left gradient bar (not a real DOM element) — opacity:0 at rest, opacity:1 on hover, animated on .message--streaming.
bubble-in and bubble-in-user animations use cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1) for a snappy overshoot-free entry — slide 6px from respective side.
rp-thought-breathe is a 5s ease-in-out opacity cycle — stops on hover to prevent interference with the hover glow state.
rules_reinforced: Token substitution in renderMarkdown() must run BEFORE marked.parse() or the marked pipeline will corrupt bracket syntax into escaped HTML entities.
::before pseudo-elements are preferred over injected DOM nodes for decorative UI elements — zero JS, zero DOM pollution.

sessions.summary: Image prompt builder overhaul. The static buildImagePrompt() was dumping raw char editor field values verbatim into prompts — narrative prose with parentheticals ('Pale grey-white (underground pallor)'), semicolons, and long descriptions. Worse, lorebook entries (world lore) were injected wholesale, polluting prompts with completely unrelated lore like 'The spice melange...' Added _cleanVisualField() to strip parentheticals/split on semicolons/cap word count before injection. Removed lorebook injection, world scenario injection, and raw chat history injection from the static path. Rewrote generateImagePromptWithLLM() with _buildCharContext() structured sheet format and a new system prompt requiring visual keywords not prose. Added describeSceneWithLLM() with charWeight/sceneWeight sliders and a collapsible Describe Scene panel in the prompt bar.
decisions: Lorebook entries removed from buildImagePrompt() entirely — they are world-knowledge prose that image models cannot use and actively harm prompt quality.
_cleanVisualField() strips (parentheticals) and splits on semicolons/commas before injecting any char editor field — prevents prose from contaminating the token stream.
describeSceneWithLLM() is a separate function from generateImagePromptWithLLM() — different use case: char-sheet-to-visual-baseline vs scene-vibe-from-history.
charWeight and sceneWeight sliders (0-1 each, default 70%/50%) let the user control how much detail the LLM allocates to character description vs environment/atmosphere.
Describe Scene panel is collapsible (hidden by default) so it doesn't add visual noise when not in use.
rules_reinforced: Never inject lorebook entries into image prompts — lorebooks contain narrative world-knowledge, not visual descriptors.
Char editor fields are written for LLM roleplay context, not image models — always translate through a keyword extractor before use in buildImagePrompt().
Separate LLM-based prompt generation (requires API call) from static keyword assembly (instant, no API) — both have different appropriate use cases.

sessions.summary: Follow-on to Wallhaven UX overhaul. Fixed Load More squishing tiles by transferring scroll ownership from .wh-grid (height:100%) to .wh-grid-area (overflow-y:auto), so the grid grows with content. Added purity-coded passive glow-pulse border animations to tiles (sfw green, sketchy amber, nsfw red; staggered 4–5s periods; hover overrides animation with sharp matching glow). Complete responsive.css rewrite: replaced all 100vh with 100dvh and -webkit-fill-available, single safe-area-inset application at .underdark-layout root, full breakpoint cascade for iPad, iPhone 14, iPhone SE, pointer:coarse touch improvements.
decisions: Scroll container for wallhaven grid moved to .wh-grid-area (overflow-y:auto); .wh-grid uses min-height:100% so it fills the area when few tiles exist but grows unbounded when Load More adds tiles.
Masonry mode removes competing overflow-y:auto (now inherits from parent scroll container).
Purity glow pulses are intentionally subtle (8-10px, 0.08-0.1 opacity at peak) — they communicate type passively without distracting from the images.
Hover on purity tiles cancels the animation (animation:none) and applies a single crisp ring glow instead — two effects at once would be chaotic.

Safe-area-inset strategy: underdark-layout absorbs all four sides once, sidebars add their lateral inset as extra padding, input zone uses max() for home-bar clearance on top of the layout root's bottom inset.
rules_reinforced: Never use height:100% on a scroll child inside overflow:hidden — the parent's height cap becomes the child's cap and content gets squished. Use min-height:100% and put overflow-y:auto on the parent.
100vh on iOS includes browser chrome (URL bar + home indicator); always use 100dvh for full-screen layouts on mobile.
Apply env(safe-area-inset-*) at exactly one place in the hierarchy — the layout root. Children that add their own safe-area padding will double-apply and clip content.

sessions.summary: Wallhaven arena full UX overhaul. Previous session (2026-05-12) had also fixed: overflow menu always visible on desktop (config-panel.css), tint picker anchor-positioning bug (openTintPicker now accepts dynamic anchor element so overflow-triggered tint popup anchors to the overflow button not hidden #chat-bg-btn), and character portrait softly behind chat tint (applyChatTint calls updateCinematicBackground when tint is active and no custom bg set). This session: mobile bottom nav moved to correct DOM position (was between header and body, now at arena bottom), full wallhaven.css overhaul (shimmer skeleton tiles, touch-device always-visible overlay, masonry via CSS columns, richer hover animations, keyboard shortcut strips), wallhaven.js bug fixes (charName changed-check was always false because WH.charName was overwritten before the comparison — fixed by capturing prevName first; auto-fetch top results when opening with no char and no prior results; blurred thumbnail placeholder in lightbox before full-res loads; preload-then-swap image reveal with animation; tileObserver rootMargin widened).
decisions: Masonry layout uses CSS columns (column-count:auto; column-width:220px) — no JS column-balancer needed, tiles break naturally.
Lightbox shows blurred thumbnail immediately on open, then swaps to full-res on load — never a blank flash.
Wallhaven auto-fetches toplist on first open with no character, so the gallery is never empty on entry.
prevName captured before WH.charName is overwritten — nameChanged check now works correctly for re-opening with same char.
Keyboard shortcut strip added to lightbox (desktop only via pointer:coarse media query) and to the empty-browse state.
Tile observer rootMargin widened to 150px 0px and root set to null (viewport) instead of the grid element — prevents tiles at edges being missed when grid has overflow:hidden.
rules_reinforced: Always capture previous state before mutating it when that previous value is needed for a comparison later in the same function.
Hidden elements (hidden attr or display:none) return zero from getBoundingClientRect — never anchor popups to elements that might be hidden; pass anchor as a parameter.
Mobile tab bars should be last in DOM flow for flex column layouts, not first.

sessions.summary: Merged the separate 'Group' button and 'New DM' button into a single thread wizard with a DM/Group toggle pill in the header. Added a fifth 'Group' tab (only visible in group mode) covering: group name/icon, memory framing (past/faint/presience/none — shapes how chars remember prior 1-on-1 DMs with you), group awareness (aware/selective/unaware — controls whether chars know others are present), turn order (auto/round-robin/player-driven), voice mode (distinct/harmonised), eight isekai world-binding questions (dynamically populated when chars are selected), character relationship web (pair-by-pair bond descriptions), and an opening scene textarea that injects a narrator system message before first_mes. All group config stored as chat.groupConfig (defaultGroupConfig() in state.js). llm-engine.js updated to inject group context (isekai answers, relationship web, awareness/voice directives) as a system message per-character and to honour the three memory framing modes with distinct narrative instructions.
decisions: defaultGroupConfig() lives in state.js — exported and imported in ui.js. createChat() auto-attaches it for group type.

TS_TABS is now dynamic (TS_TABS_DM / TS_TABS_GROUP) — _tsSwitchTab uses TS_PANEL_IDS map to find panels by ID rather than DOM order, so the group panel (id='ts-panel-group') is always correctly targeted regardless of DOM position.
_tsSetMode() swaps the tab list and re-renders the char grid (single-select vs multi-select) when the toggle is clicked.
Isekai questions and relationship web are lazily rendered when the Group tab is activated, and re-rendered whenever the character selection changes.
Memory framing 'none' skips the synapse injection entirely — clean slate, no leakage.
Group opening scene is injected as a message--system message--scene-intro element after chat creation, before first_mes.
groupConfig is passed to buildPayload via state.chat.groupConfig; llm-engine.js 2b block builds the immersion context per-character considering their specific relationship pairs and awareness mode.
Standalone #chat-new-group button removed from sidebar; New DM button relabeled 'New Thread'.

Finalization pass: threadConfig.groupTurnMode takes precedence over reality config in the group auto-responder. 'player-driven' value aliased alongside 'manual' in the responder. Group icon from groupConfig shown in chat-list avatar slot. Radio defaults reset on every modal open. Group panel rendered using TS_PANEL_IDS map (ID-based, not DOM index).
rules_reinforced: Tab switching in the wizard must use panel IDs not DOM indices — panels added out of order or conditionally hidden break index-based toggling.
Group config must live on chat.groupConfig, not threadConfig — they serve different purposes (immersion vs generation settings).
Thread-scoped config values that affect runtime behaviour (turn mode) must be read from threadConfig first, then fall back to reality config — never only from reality config.

sessions.summary: Fixed critical runtime error where _injectImageMessage and _injectVideoMessage were called in 6+ places in ui.js but never defined, causing every image-generation action to throw a ReferenceError. Both functions were implemented inside the initUI closure with full thread card layout (img frame, overlay controls: expand/fullscreen, download, save-to-gallery, delete), feeding into a new #img-msg-lightbox overlay element in index.html. addToGallery integration means every generated image auto-saves to gallery with a localPost mirror. generateImagePromptWithLLM in image-engine.js gained a withNegative:true mode that requests JSON {positive, negative} from the LLM — the image studio AI Prompt button now fills both the positive and negative fields for HiDream and other models. Image message CSS and lightbox CSS added to chat.css.
decisions: _injectImageMessage placed inside initUI closure after appendMessage so it shares $thread, addToGallery, lucideRefresh, and deleteImageMessage references without prop-drilling.
_injectVideoMessage implemented as a stub alongside _injectImageMessage — <video> element with download and delete controls.
Inline image lightbox (#img-msg-lightbox) is a singleton — src set on open, cleared on close — to avoid stacking multiple iframes.

withNegative:true returns {positive, negative} object; system prompt explicitly requests JSON; markdown fence stripping + try/catch fallback treats raw response as positive prompt so the function never throws.
Studio AI Prompt handler branches on typeof result === 'object' to handle both old string return and new {positive, negative} return without breaking existing callers.
rules_reinforced: Any function called inside initUI closure must be defined inside that same closure — no module-level declarations of UI helpers.
LLM JSON parsing must always have a try/catch fallback that degrades gracefully to string treatment.

sessions.summary: Decoupled the social feed from the gallery array. extensions.underdark.gallery now exclusively serves the lightbox and gallery strip. getAllFeedPosts() reads only from _permanentFeedPosts (feed.json) and state.socialData.localPosts. addToGallery() automatically creates a localPost entry for every image added so generated/uploaded images still appear in the feed without duplication. Gallery delete (data-del) now also calls _removeLocalPostBySrc() to keep the feed in sync. All post-count displays (sidebar, profile card, suggested list, roster profile view) updated from getAllGalleryImages to getAllFeedPosts.
decisions: Gallery array stays — it's still needed for lightbox index navigation and the gallery strip grid.
addToGallery creates the localPost *after* saving to IDB so the stored ref (not the raw dataUrl) is the src in the post.
_removeLocalPostBySrc does a src-equality lookup — works for both idb: refs and plain URLs.
Post counts in suggested lists and the feed sidebar now reflect feed.json + localPosts, not gallery size.
rules_reinforced: gallery array = lightbox/strip source only. Feed = feed.json + localPosts only. Never merge these two sources again.
Any code path that adds to the gallery must also add a localPost. Any code path that removes from gallery must also remove the localPost.

sessions.summary: Persistent social feed system added to The Underdark. New data/feed.json stores permanent posts (public image URLs or text) committed to git — visible on all instances on push. getAllFeedPosts() unifies three sources: gallery IDB images, permanent feed.json posts, and local composed posts (state.socialData.localPosts). loadFeedJson() is parallel-fetched at startup alongside loadManifest(). Compose modal added (#modal-compose-post) — character selector with avatar display, text/image-URL type tabs, caption textarea, image preview, and draft hint. #feed-add-post-btn now opens compose instead of the gallery modal. Export Feed button (#feed-export-btn) downloads all local posts (excluding IDB refs) as a dated JSON fragment ready to merge into feed.json and push. Post IDs (stable strings) replace array indices throughout comment and like state so reactions survive feed reorders.
decisions: feed.json posts[] use permanent:true — the flag is set to false on local composed posts and stripped/set true on export so the user's intent is captured at export time.
getAllFeedPosts() deduplicated by a Set of ids — safe to call multiple times without duplicating.
Gallery image posts keep postIdx:i from the gallery array for lightbox navigation; permanent and local posts use result.length as postIdx — both render correctly.
IDB refs (idb:img:*) are excluded from feed export — they are device-local blobs with no public URL and would be meaningless in git.
seedLike() now hashes the full postId string rather than (charId+index) — likes survive any reshuffling of posts.
submitSocialComment and all socialData keying migrated from postIdx (integer, unstable) to postId (string, stable).
_feedJsonCache is module-level; _permanentFeedPosts is closure-level inside initUI — bridged by assigning after both startup promises resolve.
Compose modal event listeners use {once:true} to avoid listener accumulation on repeated opens.
rules_reinforced: Any post content that lives in IDB must never be exported to a git-committed file — IDB is device-local.
Post identity must be a stable string ID, not an array index — indexes shift when posts are added/removed.
data/ JSON files are the canonical source for permanent, multi-instance-visible content.

sessions.summary: Comprehensive immersive RP UX pass on The Underdark. Six targeted improvements across ui.js and CSS: (1) dynamic input placeholder updates to 'Message {charName}…' when a character is active; (2) Quick Reply bar redesigned from 8 flat prompts to 4 themed groups (Pacing / Mood / Voice / Format) × 4 entries each, with OOC cursor-placement behaviour; (3) Scene Codex meters and digest now update unconditionally after every bot message, not just when the codex panel is open; (4) Streaming state shows animated dot-pulse + pulsing character name in the bot-label area; (5) RP text layer receives distinct visual treatment — spoken dialogue in warm amber glow (.rp-speech), inner thought in violet shimmer (.rp-thought), action/narration in muted silver (em), bold in accent-tinted white; (6) Message entrance animations differentiated — bot messages slide from left, user messages from right, both with spring-eased cubic-bezier.
decisions: _updateInputPlaceholder() added as a private helper in ui.js — called from both renderActiveBots() and setSendState() to keep placeholder in sync during idle and streaming states.
QR_GROUPS replaces flat QR_PROMPTS — grouped array with {label, entries[]} shape; _qrLookup flat map rebuilt from it for the click handler.
OOC entry appends '[OOC: ]' and places cursor inside the brackets via setSelectionRange() — not a full injected text.
Codex always-update: removed the `scene-codex--open` guard from the triggerBotResponse completion callback.
setSendState() now injects raw HTML for the bot-label during streaming (bot-label--streaming + bot-label-dots spans) — restored to plain textContent on stream end.
Message animations: msgSlideIn (bot: +translateX(-4px)) vs msgSlideInUser (user: +translateX(4px)) with identical Y offset and spring easing.
rp-speech and rp-thought hover states add extra glow — subtle but rewards mouse users without affecting mobile.
Paragraph margin added to .message--bot .message__content p for readability in longer bot responses.
rules_reinforced: Visual distinction between dialogue, thought, and action is core to immersive RP — never flatten them to the same colour/weight.
Streaming feedback must be present in the input zone, not just the message thread — the user needs to know who is writing.
QR prompts should be evocative and specific to RP context, not generic 'continue' / 'summarise' labels.

sessions.summary: Two-part gallery+avatar bug fix. (1) Social/hot feed showed broken images because _renderPostList used raw idb:img: refs directly in innerHTML — fixed by adding async resolveImageUrl pass before building HTML. (2) Avatar cache never invalidated on change and getAvatarUrl couldn't resolve idb:img: refs (only the avatar-slot format) — fixed by updating the cache key strategy and routing idb:img: refs through resolveImageUrl. Also added live feed refresh after all image generation paths and cache busting in both set-avatar paths.
decisions: Avatar cache now keyed as charId → ref (for staleness check) + charId:url → resolved URL (for return value), allowing invalidation without losing the ability to detect unchanged refs.
idb:img: and legacy idb: avatar refs handled in one function — no separate code paths needed by callers.
Feed refresh after generation gated on $feedArena.hidden — avoids unnecessary re-renders.
rules_reinforced: idb:img: (gallery images) and idb: (avatar slot) are distinct IDB stores with different lookup keys — never mix them.
Any cache keyed by charId must be invalidated whenever that character's avatar changes.

sessions.summary: Fixed generated images not appearing in the social feed, hot feed, or character gallery view. Root cause: _renderPostList built innerHTML synchronously from raw gallery refs which can be idb:img: IndexedDB references — passing those literally as produced broken images. Fixed by adding an async resolve pass (Promise.all over resolveImageUrl) before building the HTML, with filtering of any refs that fail to resolve. Also added live feed refresh after every image generation path so new images appear in the social/hot feed immediately without navigation.
decisions: Resolution happens inside _renderPostList so both renderSocialFeed and renderHotFeed benefit from a single fix.
Posts that fail IDB resolution are filtered out rather than shown as broken images.
Feed refresh after generation is gated on $feedArena visibility — no pointless re-renders when social panel isn't open.
Gallery modal upload paths (file + URL) do not need live feed refresh — the modal overlays the feed anyway.
rules_reinforced: idb:img: refs must always be passed through resolveImageUrl before use in DOM — never assume a gallery ref is a valid URL.
Any code path that mutates a character's gallery array should call renderGalleryStrip + optionally refresh the social feed if visible.

sessions.summary: Massively expanded the Image Studio Quick tab preset library. Scene presets restructured from 18 flat entries into 6 themed groups (~60 presets total: Portrait & Editorial, Lifestyle & Setting, Intimate & Sensual, Power & Dark, Fantasy & Sci-Fi, Action & Drama). Three new preset sections added: Mood Presets (18 atmosphere-only entries), Outfit Presets (20 clothing-combo entries), Activity Presets (20 pose+cam+focus entries). Relationship Dynamic expanded from 10 to 20 entries. Mood/Outfit/Activity presets apply additively (noReset) so they layer on top of existing scene state without wiping it.
decisions: Scene presets use a grouped structure (scenes.groups[]) — _renderScenePresetCards updated to handle both grouped and flat formats for forward compatibility.
Mood/Outfit/Activity presets pass noReset:true to _applyStudioScenePreset, keeping them additive — a user can set a scene preset then layer a mood on top without losing the scene.
Factored _makePresetCard() out of _renderScenePresetCards to eliminate code duplication across 4 render functions.
Group label (.studio-preset-group-label) and group row (.studio-preset-group-row) CSS classes added to img-studio.css to preserve grid layout within groups.
rp-gen-presets.json version kept at 1.0.0 — no consumer breaks, purely additive new top-level keys.
rules_reinforced: Preset data is pure JSON — all rendering code lives in ui.js and reads from rp-gen-presets.json. No preset logic hardcoded in HTML.
Additive vs reset behaviour is a caller decision at the render level, not baked into the apply function.

sessions.summary: Dead code removal and utility consolidation pass on playground/characters. Deleted char-creator.js and sims-editor.js (both superseded by char-editor.js, neither imported anywhere). Deleted creator.css, sims.css, and image-gen.css (styled modals removed in a prior commit). Created shared-utils.js exporting esc, debounce, slugify, generateId, pick, showToast — previously defined 3-6 times each across modules. Updated char-editor.js and commands.js to import from it. Renamed btn-sims-edit to btn-char-edit in HTML and JS.
decisions: wallhaven.js left as an IIFE — converting it to an ES module to use shared-utils would be a riskier refactor with no functional benefit.
ui.js showToast kept as local def (3000ms default vs 3500ms in shared-utils) — it owns the toast container and is called most frequently.

sessions.summary: Replaced the flat API key gate with a 4-step ritual initiation wizard for The Underdark. Step 0 is an atmospheric intro (animated multi-ring sigil, glitch title with CSS pseudo-element chromatic aberration, lore text). Step 1 is authentication. Step 2 is a curated model picker (12 models in a 2-col card grid) and a content threshold range dial (0–100 mapped to nsfwBypass + inject flags). Step 3 is identity (name + persona). Navigation includes forward/back, rune progress track, and a portal-drain entry animation per step. When the key is cleared post-init, gate re-opens at step 1 to skip the intro.

Model picker uses card buttons (not <select>) — 12 curated models, 2-col grid, scrollable at max-height 220px.
Content threshold is a single 0–100 range dial mapping to 4 labelled zones: Tasteful / Balanced / Explicit / Unfiltered. Sets nsfwBypass string + injectAdult flag in state.config.
Rune progress track uses ⬡ hex glyphs — active rune glows violet, done runes turn amber, connector lines animate to amber.
Step 0 is the intro — only shown on first-ever open. updateApiStatus re-opens at step 1 (skip intro) if key is cleared post-setup.
Model + threshold + identity saved to state.config via setConfig on finish, then synced to existing #model-select and #user-name-input if rendered.
Glitch effect uses CSS ::before/::after with attr(data-text) and clip-path polygon slicing — fires every 7s, no JS required.
rules_reinforced: Wizard CSS lives in api-gate.css (already a separate stylesheet per existing architecture).
No JS framework — all step logic is vanilla DOM, array indexing into querySelectorAll results.
Reduced-motion media query applied to all animations in api-gate.css.

Click fires --firing class first (300ms animation window) then navigates — gives the drain implosion time to complete.
Existing back-links (art header back-link, playground topbar, status back-nav) preserved — uplink node is supplementary, not replacement.

Z-index layers must be checked per-page before setting a fixed overlay — never assume 9999 is safe.

sessions.summary: Fixed three bugs in the Image Studio that caused character info to not load and clothing fields to be silently dropped. (1) _renderStudioCharInfo was synchronous — if the character card wasn't in loadedCharacters yet the info strip showed blank. Fixed by making it async and calling loadCharacterCard() before reading state. (2) _studioRebuildPrompt was collapsing clothingTop/Bottom/Footwear into the clothing field via || join — causing them to be dropped when clothing was also selected. Fixed by passing all three as native fields. (3) Relationships preset was adding r.accessories to the Set even when the value was an empty string, polluting the prompt. Fixed with a non-empty string guard.
decisions: _renderStudioCharInfo is now async and awaited in openImgStudio — char info and prompt rebuild both depend on the card being loaded.
clothingTop/clothingBottom/clothingFootwear are passed as native fields in the merged scene object — not collapsed into clothing.
Relationship preset accessories guard: only add to Set if r.accessories is a non-empty string after trim.
rules_reinforced: Any function that reads state.loadedCharacters must guard against the character not yet being loaded — call loadCharacterCard() first.
Studio merged scene must pass all per-part clothing fields as separate keys — never collapse them before buildImagePrompt sees them.

sessions.summary: Rewrote buildImagePrompt() in image-engine.js to correctly order prompt tokens for image diffusion models. Adult anatomy is now inline with the subject block (position 1), per-part clothing fields (clothingTop/Bottom/Footwear) are natively additive and never dropped, historyDepth:0 is properly respected for quick-capture modes, and the negative prompt was significantly expanded with the most common image generation failure modes.
decisions: Adult anatomy fields moved to position 1 (inline with subject physical block) — image diffusion models (FLUX, HiDream, SD) weight early tokens most heavily; placing anatomy after style tags caused models to anchor to style first.

clothingTop/clothingBottom/clothingFootwear are now native separate fields in buildImagePrompt — they combine additively; selecting multiple per-part fields alongside an outfit type all appear together in the prompt.
if (historyDepth > 0) guard added — quick-capture calls with historyDepth:0 now correctly exclude chat context from the prompt.
Negative prompt expanded to 5 multi-line segments covering quality, anatomy, hands/fingers (the most common artifact), duplicate bodies, watermarks/text.
Quality baseline for unrestricted mode set to: 'masterpiece, best quality, highly detailed, sharp focus, anatomically correct, perfect anatomy, 8k uhd'.
New ov() helper replaces repeated inline ternary — trims value and treats 'n/a' as empty string.
bodyFocus moved to after camera (position 9) — it is a composition/framing modifier, not a subject property.
NSFW tag placed after art style (position 18) — content modality follows style establishment.
rules_reinforced: Token ordering in image prompts follows subject → physical → clothing → action → camera → environment → style → NSFW tag. Adult anatomy is part of physical — never append it after style.
Per-part clothing fields are always additive. Never short-circuit them with || against the outfit-type field.
historyDepth is a contract — if the caller passes 0 it must be respected. Quick-capture modes depend on this.

Existing compact #modal-image-gen kept as 'Quick Gen' — studio header has a 'Quick Gen' button to swap between the two.
rp-gen-presets.json is fetched once at first open (lazy) and cached in _studioPresets — no bundle cost at boot.
Chip rendering is fully data-driven from the JSON — _renderChips() / _renderGroupedChips() write DOM from presets entries. No per-chip HTML in index.html except grouping wrappers.
Character info strip auto-fills portrait, name, hair/eye/age/species/body traits from char override on every open.
Studio scene state (_studioScene) is a superset of _scene — includes clothing top/bottom/footwear as separate fields. buildImagePrompt receives a merged view.
Gallery strip inside studio shows last 20 char images; click-to-load lets the user select an existing image as the current preview.
S key opens the studio. Existing quick modal stays on the toolbar image-plus icon.
_studioWired flag prevents double-wiring of nav and quick-capture event listeners on repeated opens.
Activity section uses grouped sub-headers (Solo/Oral/Penetrative/Finishing/BDSM) — rendered via _renderGroupedChips with per-group labels, matching the herahaven.ai UX pattern.
rules_reinforced: All chip populations are data-driven — no hardcoded chip HTML for studio. Presets JSON is the single source of truth for chip values.
Studio uses opacity transition (not display toggle) for open/close — consistent with scene-codex pattern.
Every new overlay must wire close on Escape via existing keydown handler or its own listener — studio closes on X click only currently (intentional: prevent accidental close).

for_new_llm.read_this_first: You are about to work on trap.lol. The file you are reading is status/logs/logs.json at the root of the trap.lol repository. It is the site's persistent memory and single source of truth. Read it in full before touching any code. The _protocol section is your operating instructions — it tells you exactly what to update and when. The rules and anti_patterns sections are load-bearing — violating them will produce work that gets reverted. Your last act in every session is to update this file (status/logs/logs.json) and commit it.
the_five_laws: 1. No frameworks. No build steps. Vanilla only.
2. All data lives in JSON. All HTML is scaffolding.
3. esc() on all JSON data going into innerHTML. DOMPurify on markdown.
4. lucide.createIcons() only after all HTML is injected.
5. The aesthetic is Dune × Arcane. Dark, glowing, precise, poetic. Never corporate, never default.
for_new_llm.the_sixth_law: 6. Update this file (logs.json) at the end of every session. Prepend to commits[]. Prepend to sessions[]. Update _meta.last_updated. This is not optional.
how_to_help_effectively: When asked to add a new section or item: edit the relevant JSON file. Do not touch the HTML.
When asked to add a new page: follow the pattern of the nearest existing page. Match its CSS variable usage, its glass morphism, its mono typography.
When asked to fix a bug: read the file top to bottom first. The architecture is intentional. The bug is almost never where you think it is.
When asked to improve performance: look for DOM thrashing, unnecessary re-renders, unscoped lucide.createIcons() calls, unscoped IntersectionObserver selectors.
When asked to improve UI: the answer is almost always 'add a glow' or 'add a sweep' or 'add an entrance animation'. The palette is fixed. Don't introduce new colours.
When unsure about tone for UI copy: terse. mono. ALL CAPS for states. lowercase for whispers. Never full sentences in labels.
At session end: update logs.json per _protocol before committing.
what_trap_values_most: Sovereignty — full control, no external dependencies beyond what's necessary
Precision — every pixel intentional, every byte justified
Aesthetic consistency — the palette and feel must be coherent across all pages
Speed of iteration — ship and refine, don't over-architect
Security at the boundary — validate user input, sanitize rendered content, expose nothing accidentally
Surprise — the gacha pull, the terminal shortcut system, the cursor orb — the site rewards exploration
Memory — the logs system exists because trap doesn't want to re-explain the same context every session. Honour it.

pending_work._note: This section tracks features requested by trap that are partially or fully unimplemented. An LLM reading this file must check this section before asking what to do. Status values: 'not_started' | 'in_progress' | 'done' | 'deferred' | 'wont_do'. Update status and notes after each session. Never remove entries — only update them.

system_audit.description: A comprehensive point-in-time analysis of the project's architecture, intents, strengths, and vulnerabilities.

directory_intents.glass/: The structural heart of the main hub. Contains CSS modules, vanilla JS logic, and the flat JSON files that drive the hub's UI (apps, tools, bots, hosting). It is intended to be completely static and declarative.
directory_intents.playground/characters/: The Underdark. A highly complex, isolated sub-app for LLM roleplay. Intended to handle dynamic character loading (V2 spec), sliding context windows, image/video generation, and local persistence. It is the most actively developed and volatile directory.
directory_intents.art/: ECHOES gallery. Intended for immersive, high-aesthetic artwork viewing with a hidden gacha pull mechanic. Self-contained styling and logic.
directory_intents.status/: Uplink Sentinel and the Logs terminal. Intended to probe infrastructure health and host the site's self-referential memory (logs.json).
directory_intents.guide/: Markdown reader for documentation. Intended to securely parse and render .md files with DOMPurify.
directory_intents.radar/: Torrent and TorBox integration. Intended to interface securely with external debrid APIs.

what_it_does_well: Speed & Simplicity of Deployment: The zero-build, pure vanilla architecture means changes are instantly live. No NPM install, no Webpack compilation.
Sovereign Integrity: Hard reliance on local storage and direct API routing (nano-gpt) keeps user data off intermediary servers.
Aesthetic Cohesion: The Dune x Arcane styling is flawlessly maintained across diverse sub-apps via strict CSS variable usage.
Extensibility: Adding new content (bots, tools, characters) is entirely data-driven via JSON, requiring zero HTML/JS changes.
AI Modularity: The prompt engine in The Underdark seamlessly weaves sliding history, physical anchors, and recursive summaries.
known_bugs_and_weaknesses: Vanilla DOM Desync: Lacking a framework like React/Vue, complex state changes (especially in The Underdark's Scene Codex and Image Studio) rely on manual DOM manipulation. This risks UI desyncs if elements are updated out of order.

Storage Quotas: Heavy reliance on localStorage for chat history, character states, and logs API keys. Prolonged RP sessions with multiple characters could hit the 5MB browser limit, requiring a shift to IndexedDB.

Lucide Icon Fragility: The strict rule to call lucide.createIcons() only after HTML injection works for static pages, but dynamic sub-apps require careful scoped reinjection ({ nodes: [...] }) to avoid massive performance hits or missing icons.

Mobile Layout Density: As The Underdark gains god-view panels, video polling, and image studio modals, the mobile UI is becoming increasingly crowded and difficult to navigate without overlapping absolute positioning.
Video Polling Inaccuracy: Nano-GPT video generation lacks precise progress percentages, forcing the UI to rely on heuristic time-elapsed estimation.
